        "url": "https://www.aplaceinthesun.com/articles/2025/06/a-guide-to-spanish-tax-in-2025",
        "title": "A Guide to Spanish Tax in 2025",
        "description": "### Income Tax
[...]
Income in Spain is divided into general income and savings income, each subject to different progressive rates, which are the same across the country.
[...]
2025 Savings Income Rates:
[...]
Up to €6,000
[...]
19%
[...]
€6,000 to €50,000
[...]
21%
[...]
€50,000 to €200,000
[...]
23%
[...]
€200,000 to €300,000
[...]
27%
[...]
€300,000 onwards
[...]
30%
[...]
### Personal Income Tax (General)
[...]
All other income (including rental and pension income) is classified as general income and the scale rates applied are made up of state tax rates and regional
[...]
rates, so that there are variations over the regions – whilst these are up to date at the time of writing, check for any adjustments.
[...]
This is instead of paying the progressive tax rates mentioned above that can go up to 54% - plus it exempts you from paying Wealth Tax (see below).
[...]
### Wealth and Solidarity Taxes
[...]
Spain applies two taxes on wealthy: wealth tax and solidarity tax – but you don’t pay both as any wealth tax paid is deducted from the solidarity tax liability. Solidarity tax only applies to those with net wealth above €3m, and because there are individual and main-home deductions, in reality this is €4m (or €8m for married couples).
[...]
Wealth tax applies if you owe tax after deductions or if their assets exceed €2m. Progressive rates range from 0.2% up to 3.5% and each person gets €700,000 allowance plus another €300,000 for the main home if they are resident.
[...]
Note that different regions offer different allowances – check with your advisor. Madrid currently applies a 100% relief of the wealth tax due, which effectively means zero wealth tax for residents of the region.
[...]
### Succession Tax
[...]
Recent reforms to succession (inheritance) tax have significantly improved the inheritance tax situation for spouses and children of expats in many popular regions. Andalucía, Balearic Islands, Canary Islands, Madrid, Murcia and Valenciana have all virtually eliminated inheritance tax for spouses and children – but seek specialist advice on your own situation.",
        "position": 1
      },
      {
        "url": "https://bm.consulting/en/insights/income-tax-campaign-2025-guide",
        "title": "Income Tax Campaign 2025 Spain: Complete Guide and Tax Developments",
        "description": "The Renta 2025 income tax campaign is filed between April and June 2026 and covers income, capital gains and losses incurred during the 2025 tax year. Although Spain's income tax framework has not undergone a structural overhaul between 2024 and 2025, the 2025 tax year consolidates a set of accumulated legislative changes affecting employment income, the treatment of rented residential property, crypto asset taxation and energy efficiency deductions. This guide covers the most relevant issues for taxpayers, sole traders, company shareholders and directors who must file their return in 2026.
[...]
The obligation to file applies, as a general rule, to tax residents in Spain who have received income above the following thresholds:
[...]
Investment income, property investment income, capital gains and losses subject to withholding: threshold of €1,600 per year in aggregate for these types of income, provided they have been subject to withholding.
[...]
housing purchase grants
[...]
Income not subject to withholding: taxpayers with investment income, capital gains or any non-withheld income above €500 per year must file.
[...]
- Those who have made pension plan contributions eligible for base reduction
- Those applying certain deductions (investment in new company shares, film production, double taxation relief)
- Those subject to the special impatriate regime (Beckham Law)
- Those with foreign asset declaration obligations (Form 720 or 721)
- Sole traders and business owners receiving any amount of business income
[...]
Consolidation of the 47% top marginal rate
[...]
The 47% top marginal rate for employment and business income above €300,000, introduced in 2021 and extended in subsequent years, applies in full for the 2025 tax year. Combined with the 28% savings rate for investment income above €300,000, this creates a strong incentive for high-income taxpayers and owner-directors to review their overall remuneration structure.
[...]
Owner-directors of Spanish companies face a specific challenge in the 2025 income tax return: the interaction between their personal income tax return and the company’s Corporate Income Tax return.
[...]
Remuneration structuring. Salary paid by the company to a sole shareholder-director is deductible for the company (reducing its CIT base) but taxable employment income for the individual. Dividends are not deductible for the company but are taxed in the savings base at 19–28% — typically lower than the marginal employment income rate for high earners.
[...]
Deemed income from related-party transactions. The AEAT continues to scrutinise transactions between owner-directors and their companies, particularly: loans with below-market interest rates, free use of company-owned assets, and services provided by the director at non-arm’s-length prices. These may be reclassified as deemed income or non-deductible distributions.
[...]
- All income sources are correctly reflected (employment, investment, rental, business)
- All deductible business expenses have been entered (for sole traders)
- Social Security contributions paid during 2025 are included
- The Social Security regularisation top-up (if applicable) is deducted
- All applicable deductions have been applied: energy efficiency works, charitable donations, investment in new companies, pension contributions, housing purchase (for pre-2013 mortgages)",
        "position": 2
      },
      {
        "url": "https://tranio.com/spain/taxes/",
        "title": "Property taxes in Spain in 2026: tax rate for non-resident",
        "description": "Income tax on property for non-residents (Impuesto sobre la Renta de no Residentes, IRNR). Non-resident property owners must pay tax on income acquired from their property in Spain, even if it is not rented out. In such a case, income tax is calculated at the rate of ordinary income tax. The tax rates are the following:
[...]
Capital gains tax (Impuesto sobre la Venta de Inmuebles) is applied to property sellers. The tax base is the difference between the purchase and sale price of an estate. The rate for non-residents is fixed. Starting in 2025, the rate has been increased to 30% for citizens of non-EU countries. If the owner is a Spanish resident, the tax rate is calculated in proportion to the amount of capital gains on a progressive scale from 19% to 45%.
[...]
### Tax in Spain on inheritance and donation of property
[...]
Inheritance tax (Impuesto de Sucesiones) is paid upon transfer of property, regardless of the degree of kinship. For non-residents, the tax must be paid only in case of transfer of ownership on property located in Spain. Residents of Spain must pay tax for the inheritance of any domestic and foreign properties. The rate varies from 7.65% to 40% depending on the property value.
[...]
## Income tax
[...]
Income tax in Spain is calculated at a progressive rate from 19 to 45%. Residents of Spain must pay tax on all income acquired in Spain and outside the country. The 19% rate is applied if the amount of income does not exceed 12,450 euros per year, 24% — on the income ranging from 12,450 to 20,200 euros, then 30% — in the range between 20,200 and 35,200 euros, and 37% — in the range from 35,200 to 60,000 euros. If the income exceeds 60 thousand euros, the tax rate will be 45%.
[...]
Non-residents owning property in Spain must pay income tax in this country if they receive income from renting out Spanish property. The tax has a fixed rate of 24%.
[...]
### Tax rates in Spain
[...]
Inheritance tax
[...]
7.65–40
[...]
19–45
[...]
Tax on income from property (
[...]
the property is
[...]
- VAT is 10% or 21% (for commercial property).
- The tax on the transfer of property rights is from 6 to 11%.
- Registration fee (Stamp duty) — from 0.5 to 2%.
- The municipal property tax is from 0.4 to 1.1%.
- The luxury tax for property values over 700 thousand euros ranges from 0.2 to 3.5%.
- Capital gains tax — from 19 to 45%.
- Inheritance tax — from 7.65 to 40%.
- Income tax is from 19 to 45%.
- The tax on income from property, if it is rented out, is 24%.
- The tax on income from property, if it is not rented, is 24% or 1.1% of the cadastral value of the object.
[...]
the same way as income tax
[...]
at a progressive rate. In case of absence of residency: if
[...]
a fixed tax of 24% of
[...]
does not rent
[...]
24%
[...]
1% of",
        "position": 3
      },
      {
        "url": "https://theglobalwealth.com/guides/spain-taxes",
        "title": "Spain Taxes: A Complete Guide to the Spanish Tax System - The Global Wealth",
        "description": "Impuesto sobre
[...]
Renta de las Person
[...]
For 2026, the state-level tax brackets are structured as follows. Income up to EUR 12K is taxed at 19%, income between EUR 12K and EUR 20K at 24%, and income between EUR 20K and EUR 35K at 30%.
[...]
The rates continue to increase for higher income levels. Income between EUR 35K and EUR 60K is taxed at 37%, between EUR 60K and EUR 300K at 45%, and any income exceeding EUR 300K is taxed at the top rate of 47%.
[...]
## Wealth Tax and Solidarity Tax
[...]
Spain maintains two separate taxes on accumulated wealth. The traditional wealth tax (Impuesto sobre el Patrimonio) has existed for decades, while the newer solidarity tax on large fortunes was introduced in December 2022.
[...]
The wealth tax is an annual levy on the net value of personal assets as of December 31 each year. Spanish tax residents are taxed on their worldwide assets, while non-residents are taxed only on Spanish-situs assets.
[...]
Each individual receives a personal allowance of EUR 700K , plus an additional EUR 300K exemption for their main home in Spain. This means most residents can hold up to EUR 1.0M in net assets before owing any wealth tax.
[...]
The solidarity tax was specifically designed to counteract regional wealth tax exemptions. Unlike the traditional wealth tax, this levy applies uniformly across all of Spain, including regions that have eliminated their wealth tax.
[...]
The solidarity tax applies to net wealth exceeding EUR 3.0M . The rate is 1.7% for wealth between EUR 3.0M and EUR 5.3M , 2.1% for wealth between EUR 5.3M and EUR 10.7M , and 3.5% for wealth above EUR 10.7M .
[...]
Originally introduced as a temporary measure in 2023, the Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes was made permanent in 2025. This effectively overrides regional wealth tax exemptions for high-net-worth individuals with assets exceeding EUR 3.0M , meaning residents of Madrid and Andalusia with wealth above this threshold still face wealth taxation at the national level.
[...]
## Inheritance and Gift Tax
[...]
tax in Spain varies dramatically depending on the autonomous community where the recipient resides or where the assets are located. This creates one of the most complex areas of Spanish taxation.
[...]
The national tax rate ranges from 7.65% to 34% before applying regional reductions. Higher-value inheritances fall into higher tax brackets, with rates increasing based on both the amount inherited and the relationship between the deceased and the heir.
[...]
Andalusia offers a EUR 1.0M exemption per heir along with a 99% tax reduction for close family members (Groups I and II). Madrid provides a 99% rebate for spouses, descendants, and ascendants, significantly reducing the tax burden for direct heirs.
[...]
The Canary Islands offer even more generous treatment with a 99.9% reduction. In contrast, regions such as Asturias and Catalonia maintain higher rates and lower tax benefits, which can considerably increase the cost of an inheritance.
[...]
Autonomous communities can modify personal income tax rates, wealth tax rates and exemptions, inheritance and
[...]
tax rates, and various deductions and credits. They cannot, however, modify VAT rates or corporate tax rates, which remain uniform nationwide.
[...]
Madrid consistently ranks as the most tax-f
[...]
region for high earners and high net worth individuals
[...]
Its combination of lower
[...]
tax exemptions creates the lowest overall tax burden.
[...]
Spain's tax system is multilayered and varies significantly between regions. Progressive income tax rates reach 47% at the national level, but regional variations can increase or decrease your total tax burden.
[...]
Special regimes like the Beckham Law provide valuable opportunities for expatriates, while the combination of wealth tax and solidarity tax creates challenges for high net worth individuals. Inheritance and gift tax treatment depends heavily on which autonomous community you or your heirs reside in.",
        "position": 4
      },
      {
        "url": "https://taxatlas.io/country/spain",
        "title": "Spain Tax Rates & System (2026) — TaxAtlas",
        "description": "Spain operates a progressive income tax system with federal rates from 19% to 47%, supplemented by regional surcharges that vary across its 17 autonomous communities. The country features a 25% corporate tax rate, a 21% standard VAT, and a special Beckham Law regime allowing qualifying new residents to pay a flat 24% rate on Spanish-source income. Spain also levies both wealth tax and a temporary solidarity tax on large fortunes.
[...]
Spain applies a progressive personal income tax (IRPF - Impuesto sobre la Renta de las Personas Físicas) with general rates ranging from 19% to 47% at the national level. These rates are split between the state and regional governments (approximately 50/50). Spain's 17 autonomous communities can adjust their portion of the rates, resulting in effective top rates varying from about 45% to over 54% depending on the region. Catalonia and Valencia have some of the highest regional surcharges, while Madrid has among the lowest.
[...]
| Income Range | Tax Rate |
| --- | --- |
| €0 – €12K | 19% |
| €12K – €20K | 24% |
| €20K – €35K | 30% |
| €35K – €60K | 37% |
| €60K – €300K | 45% |
| €300K+ | 47% |
[...]
Residency RuleIndividuals spending more than 183 days in Spain per calendar year, or whose center of vital interests or economic activity is in Spain, are considered tax residents. Residents are taxed on worldwide income. Spain also has an anti-avoidance rule presuming residency if the taxpayer's spouse and minor children reside in Spain.
[...]
Does Spain have a wealth tax?",
        "position": 5
      },
      {
        "url": "https://www.healthplanspain.com/blog/expat-tips/2208-spain-tax-reforms-2025-expats.html",
        "title": "Spain’s Autumn 2025 Tax Reforms: Essential Guide for Expats, Retirees, and Property Owners",
        "description": "- Marginal rates: Earnings up to €12,450 taxed at 19%, then at 24% for earnings to €20,200, rising in bands up to 47% for income above €300,000.
- Savings income: Capital gains, dividends, and certain investment returns taxed at 30% for sums above €300,000 (previously 28%), and 19–27% below this.
- Thresholds & returns: Personal returns required for total annual income over €15,000, with additional forms (rental, freelance, foreign assets) as needed.
[...]
- Beckham Law: Qualifying expats and foreign professionals now pay a flat 24% tax on Spanish earnings up to €600,000 (47% above that), with non-Spanish income remaining exempt. Key change: All applications and renewals now online; UK workers and digital nomads can qualify if hired or self-employed in Spain for at least six months.
[...]
## Inheritance and Succession Tax: Regional Revolution
[...]
- Nil-rate bands: Most heirs in Andalusia, Madrid, and Valencia pay zero inheritance tax on bequests up to €1,000,000 (per beneficiary) if inheriting from parents, grandparents, or spouses. In Catalonia and Balearics, the band is lower (€650,000).
- Cross-border estates: Dual nationals and non-residents should review succession tax rules—inheritance tax must be paid in Spain before assets can be distributed abroad, and UK/Spanish double-tax treaties may apply.
- Gifts and distant relatives: Siblings, nephews/nieces, and non-family face variable rates (5–37%), with many regions increasing relief for small inheritances and reducing paperwork.
- Pro tip: Regional rules change often; consult a succession lawyer for updated guidance.
[...]
## Wealth, Corporate, and Non-Resident Taxes
[...]
- Wealth Tax: Residents with assets over €700,000 (excluding primary home up to €300,000) are liable. Many regions offer extra relief for pensioners and first-time buyers.
- Corporate Rate: Micro-enterprises (revenue below €1m) enjoy 21% on first €50,000 profit, and 22% thereafter, with capitalisation reserve deductions encouraging reinvestment.
- Rental Income: Expats renting out properties must register with Hacienda, pay IRPF, and declare rental income separately. New forms and digital signatures required.
[...]
- Q: Do expats pay more inheritance tax? A: No—same nil-rate bands apply to most residents.
[...]
- Q: If I own property through a company, do I still pay IRPF? A: Yes—both owner and company must document rental income and expenses.",
        "position": 6
      },
      {
        "url": "https://koronapay.com/transfers/europe/en/blog/taxes-in-spain-in-2025/",
        "title": "Taxes in Spain in 2025 for Residents and Non-Residents",
        "description": "In 2025, Spain maintains a clear system for
[...]
tax residency. This status defines what kind of taxes you’ll
[...]
on income earned within
[...]
If a person qualifies as a tax resident, they must declare and pay taxes on all income worldwide — including salaries, business profits, and investments. Residents are subject to a progressive income tax scale: from 19% on lower incomes to 47% on amounts exceeding €300,000 per year. Residency status also affects property and inheritance taxes, as well as eligibility for various deductions and benefits.
[...]
Residents pay IRPF (Impuesto sobre la Renta de las Personas Físicas) — a personal income tax applied to worldwide income, including salaries, business profits, dividends, rental income, and other earnings.
[...]
Spain uses a progressive tax scale, meaning that higher income levels are taxed at higher rates. The exact rates may vary slightly by region, but the average national brackets are:
[...]
- up to €12,450 — 19%
- €12,450–€20,200 — 24%
- €20,200–€35,200 — 30%
- €35,200–€60,000 — 37%
- over €60,000 — 45%
[...]
For very high incomes exceeding €300,000, the rate can reach 47%.
[...]
Non-residents pay IRNR (Impuesto sobre la Renta de no Residentes) — a tax only on income earned within Spain, such as rental income, employment, or business activity in Spanish territory.
[...]
The standard tax rate for most non-residents is 24%. However, citizens of EU and EEA countries benefit from a reduced rate of 19%, provided certain conditions are met.
[...]
The main feature of the regime is that new tax residents of Spain can pay a flat income tax rate of 24% only on income earned within Spain. This is much lower than the standard progressive income tax rate, which can reach up to 47%. The benefit is valid for six years from the moment the taxpayer becomes a resident.
[...]
Under this regime, foreign income is exempt from Spanish taxation. The 24% flat rate applies to annual income up to €600,000. Any income above that threshold is taxed at 47%.
[...]
Spain has a comprehensive tax system covering real estate, corporate profits, and investment income. The exact tax obligations for foreigners depend on their residency status and the type of income they receive.
[...]
Every property owner must pay an annual property tax (IBI — Impuesto sobre Bienes Inmuebles). The rate is based on the property’s cadastral value and typically ranges from 0.4% to 1.1%.
[...]
When selling real estate, the capital gains tax applies — between 19% and 26%, depending on the amount of profit. If the property is rented out, the rental income is also taxable: 19% for EU citizens and 24% for non-EU residents.
[...]
Dividends, bank interest, and capital gains from the sale of securities are subject to progressive tax rates ranging from 19% to 26%. Non-residents generally pay the same rates on income from Spanish investments, though specific rules may vary.
[...]
In summary, we’ve reviewed the main tax rates and types in Spain. The Spanish tax system combines progressive income taxation, corporate levies, and property and investment taxes. At the same time, the government provides a wide range of tax benefits for families, entrepreneurs, and innovation-driven projects, helping to reduce the overall tax burden and encourage economic growth.
[...]
For residents, the key obligation is to pay taxes on worldwide income, while non-residents are taxed only on income earned in Spain. Therefore, before relocating or investing, it’s important to understand which tax status you’ll obtain and what responsibilities it entails.",
        "position": 7
      },
      {
        "url": "https://www.tax121.com/blog/how-wealth-tax-works-in-spain-a-complete-guide-for-2025-2026",
        "title": "How Wealth Tax Works in Spain: A Complete Guide for 2025/2026 | Tax121 Blog",
        "description": "If you own property, investments, or other significant assets in Spain, understanding Spain wealth tax explained in plain language is
[...]
for your financial planning. Spain is one of the few European countries that levies an annual tax on an individual's net worldwide wealth — and for the 2025/2026 tax year, the rules remain critically important for both residents and non-residents alike.
[...]
Spain's wealth tax — known as the Impuesto sobre el Patrimonio — is an annual tax levied on the net assets of individuals. Unlike income tax, which targets what you earn, wealth tax targets what you own as of 31 December each year.
[...]
The tax was originally introduced in 1977, temporarily abolished in 2008, and then reinstated in 2011 as a "temporary" measure during the economic crisis. It has been extended repeatedly since then and remains in force for the 2025/2026 fiscal year.
[...]
- Annual tax: Assessed every year based on your net wealth as of 31 December.
- Personal tax: Only individuals pay wealth tax; companies and other legal entities are exempt.
- Progressive rates: The more you own above the threshold, the higher the marginal rate.
- Regional variations: Spain's autonomous communities (regions) can modify rates, thresholds, and exemptions.
[...]
- General exemption: €700,00
[...]
per taxpayer on net assets
[...]
- Primary residence exemption: Up
[...]
00 of the value of your habitual residence is exempt (residents only).
[...]
## Spain Wealth Tax Rates for 2025/2026
[...]
The wealth tax rates in Spain are progressive and applied in bands. Below are the state-level rates for the 2025/2026 tax year:
[...]
| Taxable Net Wealth (€) | Marginal Rate |
| --- | --- |
| Up to 167,129.45 | 0.20% |
| 167,129.45 – 334,252.88 | 0.30% |
| 334,252.88 – 668,499.75 | 0.50% |
| 668,499.75 – 1,336,999.51 | 0.90% |
| 1,336,999.51 – 2,673,999.01 | 1.30% |
| 2,673,999.01 – 5,347,998.03 | 1.70% |
| 5,347,998.03 – 10,695,996.06 | 2.10% |
| Over 10,695,996.06 | 3.50% |
[...]
Spain applies an important cap: the combined total of your wealth tax and income tax cannot exceed 60% of your taxable income. However, even if this cap is triggered, you must still pay at least 20% of the originally calculated wealth tax. This prevents high-net-worth individuals with relatively low declared income from entirely escaping wealth tax.
[...]
- 0% on the first €3,000,000 of taxable base (after the €700,000 exemption)
- 1.7% on €3,000,000 to €5,347,998.03
- 2.1% on €5,347,998.03 to €10,695,996.06
- 3.5% on amounts exceeding €10,695,996.06
[...]
Wealth tax returns are filed annually, typically alongside your income tax return. For the 2025/2026 tax year (fiscal year ending 31 December 2025), the filing period generally runs from April to June 2026 — the Spanish tax agency (Agencia Tributaria) announces the exact dates each year.
[...]
You must file a wealth tax return
[...]
- Your gross assets exceed €2,000,000 (even if your net taxable wealth after exemptions is below the tax-free allowance), or
- Your calculated wealth tax liability is greater than zero.
[...]
deductible against income tax
[...]
No, wealth tax is not deductible from your Spanish income tax. However, the 60% combined tax cap (discussed above) limits the cumulative burden of both taxes.
[...]
1. Spain levies an annual wealth tax on individuals' net assets, with progressive rates from 0.20% to 3.50% at the state level.
[...]
2. Tax residents pay on worldwide assets; non-residents pay only on Spanish-situs assets.
[...]
3. The general exemption is €700,000, plus an additional €300,000 for your primary residence (residents only).
[...]
rates in Catalonia and Valencia.
[...]
5. The Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes creates a minimum national floor for individuals with net assets above €3,000,000.
[...]
6. The 60% combined tax cap limits the total of income tax plus wealth tax, but you must always pay at least 20% of the calculated wealth tax.",
        "position": 8
      },
      {
        "url": "https://voguimmo.com/en/80-wealth-tax-spain-rules-rates-advice.htm",
        "title": "Wealth Tax in Spain: Rules, Rates, and Tips 2025",
        "description": "In addition to local taxes (such as IBI) and income tax (IRNR), foreign property owners in Spain may be subject to wealth tax. This tax, known as "Impuesto sobre el Patrimonio" (IP), targets individuals with a high net worth. It is a critical financial matter that must be fully understood to anticipate its impact on your real estate investment.
[...]
This is an annual tax levied on net wealth. For non-residents, it applies only to assets and rights located in Spain. Its complexity stems from the fact that its rates are national, but its management is devolved to the Autonomous Communities, which can modify it significantly (as in Madrid). This guide decodes the rules, 2025 rates, and optimization strategies.
[...]
Spanish wealth tax is an annual tax levied on the net wealth of taxpayers as of December 31st each year. It applies to all assets, from which debts can be deducted.
[...]
A general allowance (exempt base) of €700,000 applies. Therefore, a non-resident is only liable for wealth tax if their net assets in Spain exceed €700,000. Tax residents also benefit from an additional €300,000 allowance on their main residence.
[...]
## Applicable Rates in 2025: The Impact of Regions
[...]
The tax is progressive and applies to the net value of assets (after the €700,000 allowance). The 2025 national rates are as follows, although they may be modified by each Autonomous Community.
[...]
| Net Taxable Base (After Allowance) | Progressive Rate (National Scale) |
| --- | --- |
| Up to €167,129.45 | 0.2 % |
| From €167,129.45 to €334,252.88 | 0.3 % |
| From €334,252.88 to €668,505.75 | 0.5 % |
| From €668,505.75 to €1,337,011.51 | 0.9 % |
| From €1,337,011.51 to €2,674,023.01 | 1.3 % |
| From €2,674,023.01 to €5,348,046.02 | 1.7 % |
| From €5,348,046.02 to €10,696,092.04 | 2.1 % |
| Over €10,696,092.04 | 3.5 % |
[...]
This is the most important point. Regions have the power to modify this tax.
[...]
- Madrid: Applies a 100% bonus. Simply put, wealth tax there is €0.
- Andalusia: Also applies a 100% bonus.
- Valencian Community & Catalonia: Apply the tax (sometimes with their own rates and allowances) and it is therefore due.
[...]
For a non-resident, the law stipulates that the state's rates apply, *unless* the region where the majority of their assets are located has less favorable rates, in which case those rates prevail.
[...]
The « Impuesto sobre el Patrimonio » (IP) is an annual tax levied on the net wealth (i.e., your assets minus your liabilities) held by a taxpayer as of December
[...]
st each year
[...]
Yes, but only on assets and rights you own within Spanish territory. This includes your real estate properties, Spanish bank accounts, or even boats. This differs from Spanish tax residents, who are taxed on their worldwide wealth.
[...]
No, probably
[...]
. You are
[...]
your net wealth in
[...]
(after deducting liabilities) exceeds the general allowance of €700,000. If your net wealth is below this threshold, you will not have to pay any tax.
[...]
That's correct. The Community of Madrid applies a 100% tax relief on this tax. In practice, even if your wealth exceeds the threshold, the final tax payable is €0. Andalusia also applies the same exemption.
[...]
Regions highly popular with international investors, such as the Valencian Community (Alicante, Valencia) and Catalonia (Barcelona), do not have this exemption and apply the tax (sometimes with their own specific rates). The choice of location is therefore crucial for tax optimization.
[...]
### What is the tax rate?
[...]
It's a progressive scale with bands, similar to income tax. The national scale starts at 0.2% (on the first band of the taxable base) and can go up to 3.5% for wealth exceeding €10 million.",

As previously announced, the forthcoming combined allowance for the 100% rate of agricultural property relief and business property relief is fixed at £1 million until 5 April 2030.         "url": "https://wedlakebell.com/insights/globally-speaking/reforms-to-the-uk-taxation-of-individuals-and-their-wealth-structures-a-summary",
        "title": "Reforms to the UK taxation of individuals and their wealth structures: A summary - Wedlake Bell",
        "description": "On 6 April 2025, significant changes to the UK tax regime for non-UK domiciled individuals (“non-doms“) and future arrivers into the UK took effect. These reforms were initially announced by the Conservative government in March 2024 but were confirmed, extended and enacted by the Labour government after their election victory in July 2024.
[...]
The previous non-dom regime, including the “remittance basis” of taxation, has its origins from 1799. The basic principle for income tax and capital gains tax (“CGT“) purposes was that non-doms were taxed on their foreign income and gains (“FIG“) only if they brought them to the UK. From 2008, non-doms had to pay an annual charge to use the remittance basis after seven years of UK residence. Further changes were made in 2017 so that a non-dom would be deemed to be UK domiciled for UK tax purposes after fifteen years of UK residence. There was also a protection regime for FIG within offshore trusts set up by a non-dom, such that the FIG could not generally be attributed to the non-dom for UK tax purposes. The basic principle for inheritance tax (“IHT“) purposes was that, provided an individual was not domiciled in the UK under general law and not deemed domiciled, their non-UK assets generally remained outside the scope of IHT. If a non-dom put assets into an offshore trust, these assets generally remained excluded from IHT (known as an “excluded property trust” – “EPT“) even if the non-dom later became UK domiciled or deemed domiciled.
[...]
Four-year exemption for income and gains: the remittance basis has been abolished and replaced with a new four-year residence-based exemption regime for FIG. This applies to individuals who become UK resident after at least ten years of non-UK residence. During the first four years, they do not pay UK tax on their FIG, including distributions from non-UK resident trusts. There is no charge for access to this exemption regime. After four years, however, if such individuals remain in the UK, they are subject to UK tax on their worldwide income and gains.
[...]
Transitional rules for income and gains: for FIG arising before 6 April 2025 and remitted to the UK after this date, those individuals who were non-doms under the old regime can elect to pay UK tax at a reduced flat rate under the Temporary Repatriation Facility (“TRF“) for tax years 2025/26, 2026/27, and 2027/28. The TRF rates are 12% in 2025/26 and 2026/27, and 15% in 2027/28. This compares with alternative rates up to 45% if the TRF was not used. Non-doms can also elect to rebase the value of non-UK assets for CGT purposes to market value as at 5 April 2017, reducing the chargeable gain upon disposal after 5 April 2025, subject to conditions.
[...]
Residence-based system for IHT: liability to IHT is now based on residence rather than domicile. Individuals who have been UK resident for ten or more of the preceding 20 tax years (a “long-term resident” – “LTR“) are subject to IHT on their worldwide assets; otherwise, those who are not LTR, are generally only subject to IHT on their UK assets (as non-doms were under the previous regime). There is a “tail” period of IHT exposure after an LTR individual leaves the UK, ranging from three to ten years.
[...]
Offshore trusts and IHT: offshore trusts that are EPTs are now
[...]
new residence-
[...]
trust assets are now

of IHT

[...]
on trust distributions

The reforms significantly alter the UK tax landscape for existing non-doms and future UK arrivers in respect of their FIG and IHT treatment. Professional advice is crucial to understand the impact and explore planning options. The four-year FIG regime is less competitive than similar regimes in other European jurisdictions, but may attract individuals for short-term residence. The new IHT regime may also potentially prompt some long-term residents to reconsider their location options.",

        "url": "https://kpmg.com/uk/en/insights/tax/tmd-budget-non-dom-reforms-are-going-ahead-from-april-2025.html",
        "title": "Budget: Non-dom reforms are going ahead from April 2025 | KPMG UK",
        "description": "This article is a ’whistle stop tour’ of the main highlights of the policy paper on the changes to the remittance basis and the move from a domicile to a residence basis of taxation.
[...]
- All UK resident individuals will pay tax on their worldwide income and gains arising after 5 April 2025 and the remittance basis will come to an end;
- There will be a four-year FIG (foreign income and gains) regime available for those who come to the UK after 10 years of non-UK residence;
- There will be a Temporary Repatriation Facility (TRF); and
- There will be some rebasing for capital gains.
[...]
In addition, as previously announced, inheritance tax (IHT) will become a residence-based system with effect from 6 April 2025 (our article ‘ The new look inheritance tax’ goes into more detail) and domicile will become a largely irrelevant concept in the tax world (bar some transitional provisions and perhaps for the purpose of some estate tax treaties). So that’s the headlines, let’s have a peer at some of the detail (a kind of helicopter view at this stage).
[...]
Qualifying individuals will be able to claim relief from UK tax for any non-UK income and gains arising in the first four years of their UK tax residence.
[...]
The four-year FIG regime will be available to those who come to the UK after a period of 10 consecutive years of non-residence and will apply from 6 April 2025. It is important to highlight that this regime can apply to so called ‘Brits abroad’ who are returning to the UK after 10 consecutive years of non-UK residence.
[...]
An individual can claim only for income, only for gains or for both. They will need to make a claim for each source of income and/or gain on which relief is being sought and will need to include amounts on their tax return. Individuals who claim relief under the four-year FIG regime will lose entitlement to the personal allowance for income tax and the annual exempt amount for capital gains tax (CGT). The technical note contains a long list of the income and gains that will qualify (or not) for the regime. Offshore income gains are included in the list of qualifying income but, perhaps unsurprisingly, gains from offshore life insurance policies will not qualify.
[...]
The policy document published confirms that there will be rebasing for personally held assets for current and past remittance users for disposals after 6 April 2025.

for a limited period of 3 tax years, from 2025 to 2026. The Temporary Repatriation Facility rate will be 12% for the first 2 years and 15% in the final tax year of operation.

The current domicile-based system of Inheritance Tax will be replaced with a new residence-based system. This will affect the scope of non-UK property brought into UK Inheritance Tax for individuals and trusts. An individual is long-term resident (and in scope for Inheritance Tax on their non-UK assets) when they have been resident in the UK for at least 10 out of the last 20 tax years and then remain in scope for between 3 and 10 years after leaving the UK. Subject to transitional points, any non-UK assets a person put into a settlement will be subject to Inheritance Tax charges at times when the settlor is long-term resident.
[...]
The government is committed to addressing unfairness in the tax system, so that everyone who is long-term resident in the UK pays their taxes here. The government will therefore remove the outdated concept of domicile status from the tax system and implement a new residence-based regime which is internationally competitive and focused on attracting the best talent and investment to the UK.
[...]
On 29 July 2024, as part of the Chancellor’s statement on the public spending inheritance, a policy document was published outlining the government’s planned changes to the taxation of non-UK domiciled individuals.
[...]
This measure will come into force on 6 April 2025 in relation to tax charges arising on or after that date.

Einen professionellen, schnellen und unkomplizierten Service als Einwanderungshelfer bieten dir Evelyn und Georg Mitrakis, die bereits seit mehreren Jahren erfolgreich im Süden Paraguays tätig sind und mit den zuständigen Behörden in den Kolonien (Hohenau, Obligado und Bella Vista) und Encarnación gut zusammenarbeiten. Sie sprechen: Deutsch, Spanisch, Englisch, Russisch und Griechisch.
[...]
Du erreichst sie unter folgender Nummer:Telegram und WhatsApp: +595 984 995 204Email soldelsur.paraguay@gmail.com
[...]
Auch wenn du Fragen an uns hast, wie das Leben hier tatsächlich ist und was es bei einer Auswanderung alles zu beachten gilt (Abmeldung, Kindergeld, Steuern, Container, Immobilien usw.), melde dich bei uns, schreib uns einfach eine Email an info@hohenau-obligado.com und wir helfen wirklich sehr gerne weiter.Tritt ein in unsere Telegram-Gruppe und tausche dich mit uns und vielen anderen aus. Hier geht´s zur Telegram-Gruppe.
[...]
Weitere Infos zu Hohenau, Obligado und Bella Vista findest Du hier:
[...]
Wenn schon Auswandern nach Paraguay, warum dann ausgerechnet nach Hohenau? Was uns an den Vereinigten Kolonien besonders gefällt, und wie es sich als Deutscher hier anfühlt, erzählen wir Euch hier.
[...]

        "title": "Auswandern Paraguay | Grundstücke & Erfahrungen | Valle Colibri",
        "description": "Auswandern Paraguay | Grundstücke & Erfahrungen | Valle Colibri
[...]
# Auswandern nach Paraguay
[...]
### Wir, Cristina und Daniel leben seit 2024 in der Cordillera. Wir teilen alles, was du für deinen Neustart brauchst – von der Auswanderung bis zum eigenen Grundstück.
[...]
Hier bei Paraguay Lifestyle teilen wir mit dir, wie das Leben in dieser Ecke Südamerikas aussieht und was du wissen musst, wenn auch du die Kontrolle über dein Leben zurückgewinnen und einen neuen Lebensstil verwirklichen möchtest.
[...]
Wir wählten die Region Corillera, die Hügellandschaft am See Ypacarai mit einer Anbindung an die Hauptstadt Asunción, um unser anspruchsvolles Abenteuer zu beginnen.
[...]
## Valle Colibri
[...]
Inmitten der hügeligen Landschaft, Cordillera genannt, liegt unser Valle Colibri mit einer fantastischen Aussicht. Wir haben uns bewusst für dieses Gebiet entschieden. Die gesamte Umgebung ist ein Traum, mitten in der Natur, aber nur wenige Meter vom Zentrum Loma Grand entfernt. In Loma Grand gibt es alles für den täglichen Bedarf. Altos, Nueva Colombia und San Bernardino sind in unmittelbarer Nachbarschaft.
[...]
## Paraguay Lifestyle
[...]
Wir arbeiten daran, Paraguay Lifestyle zum ersten paraguayischen Online-„Lifestyle“-Magazin zu machen. Mit unserer eigenen Handschrift und interessanten Informationen über aktuelle Themen, Nachrichten, Veranstaltungen, Dienstleistungen und mehr. Bei uns findest du die Stilpunkte, die du sucht und kannst du dich über alles informieren, was in unserer Nachbarschaft, auf dem Land und in der Stadt passiert.
[...]
Wir helfen dir gerne – ohne Druck, aber mit viel Erfahrung! Wir bieten dir praktische Tipps und Insiderwissen zur Einwanderung, und dank unseres Netzwerks auch mit Einheimischen, können wir dich auch optional unterstützen. Egal, ob du Fragen zu Visum, Behörden oder dem Leben vor Ort hast: Melde dich einfach bei uns – wir begleiten dich auf deinem Weg!“",
        "position": 10
      }
    ]
  }
}

Whether you have settled as an expat in Paraguay or are still living in United Kingdom and planning your upcoming foreign assignment, InterNations Paraguay offers everything you need: Established in 2007, our trusted community soon became the leading platform for international networking among expatriates and global minds around the world. Our network also counts a considerable number of British expats in Paraguay, coming from all corners of United Kingdom - from Glasgow, over London to Cardiff.
[...]
Do you want to continue with your hobbies — or discover new ones — while living abroad? There’s a variety of InterNations Groups you can join, including everything from sightseeing to hiking. These are run by dedicated Consuls with at least one activity each month, such as dinners in the cities finest restaurants, sports or outings with other expats to explore all that your new home has to
[...]
## Find Tips and Advice on Paraguay from British Expats
[...]
Of course, expat living in Paraguay is not just about shipping your belongings and filing in some bureaucratic papers. Regular socializing with fellow Brits is an essential part of expat life and helps getting accustomed to the Paraguayan way of life and mentality. InterNations helps you connect and meet up with other Brits in Paraguay, residing in Asunción, Ciudad del Este, Formosa and many other places throughout the country. Just take a look at our group of Brits in Asunción.
[...]
- Look up our top quality network to get to know open-minded expats from United Kingdom in Paraguay.
- Obtain and share insider tips in our Paraguay Expat Forums – e.g. how to hire a babysitter fluent in your native tongue for your children or where to get roast beef in Ciudad del Este.
- Search the InterNations Places database for Paraguay to get information on institutions from your home country, including the British embassy.
[...]
From the Chaco in the west to the Paraná region, we’ll help you connect with supportive and friendly British expats in Paraguay.
[...]
## Join Our Expat Activities and Mingle with Fellow Brits in Paraguay
[...]
Moreover, please feel free to attend our regular expat events and activities in Paraguay. Our get-togethers are very diverse and range from a cozy gathering to enjoy the traditional Paraguayan sopa paraguaya, to big expat celebrations with hundreds of participants in Asunción’s best locations. You can also participate in one of our expat trips to go and see Encarnación and its Jesuit missions as well as other beautiful landmarks in Paraguay.
[...]
Our meet-ups and activities are a great opportunity to mingle with expats from United Kingdom and various other countries in a casual setting. Interested? Just sign up and you’ll soon be receiving a cordial Hello from our Brits living in Paraguay!
[...]

Pour un nombre de raisons élevées (que je développerai peut-être plus tard), mon épouse et moi souhaitons partir nous installer au Paraguay d'ici début 2027. Nous allons effectuer notre premier voyage dans 2 mois pour une durée de 3 semaines. Nos enfants sont grands et quittent eux aussi la France pour des aventures sur d'autres continents. Nous aimons particulièrement la tranquillité (la foule ce n'est pas notre truc), et nous voulons profiter des années à venir pour voyager le plus possible. Je ne serai pas contre monté un business, je ne manque pas d'idées... Au plaisir de vous lire.

 

J'aime

        "title": "Cambio para acceder a nacionalidad italiana impacta “de manera fatal” a descendientes en Paraguay - Nacionales - ABC Color",
        "description": "Cambio para acceder a nacionalidad italiana impacta “de manera fatal” a descendientes en Paraguay - Nacionales - ABC Color
[...]
# Cambio para acceder a nacionalidad italiana impacta “de manera fatal” a descendientes en Paraguay
[...]
## Recientemente, el gobierno de Italia decidió limitar la ley que concede su nacionalidad a los descendientes de italianos en el extranjero. Cómo afecta esta medida en un país donde se estima que el 30% de la población tiene un ancestro llegado desde Italia.
[...]
Para evitar la “comercialización” y por un considerable aumento de las solicitudes -principalmente por parte de personas nacidas Sudamérica- el gobierno italiano cambió el requisito para acceder a su nacionalidad para los descendientes.
[...]
La ley ahora prevé que los descendientes de italianos nacidos en el extranjero solo recibirán la nacionalidad por dos generaciones. Es decir, si al menos un progenitor o un abuelo nació en Italia.
[...]
Al respecto, Waldo Emerson Viapiana, presidente del “Gruppo Noi Siamo”, que nuclea a paraguayos con ascendencia italiana, manifestó que la medida impacta “de manera fatal” para los paraguayos descendientes de italianos.
[...]
“Es abrumador para mucha gente; es más abrumador para las personas que estuvieron haciendo los trámites y estuvieron gastando millones con los documentos”, detalló en comunicación con ABC Color.
[...]
## Cambio para
[...]
Según explicó Viapiana, hasta el 28 de marzo pasado Italia otorgaba su nacionalidad “sin límite generacional” mientras se podía demostrar el vínculo. “Eso cambió inesperadamente”, agregó.
[...]
“Aquellos que ya obtuvimos (la nacionalidad italiana) no vamos a poder transmitirla a nuestros hijos a menos que vayamos a vivir dos años en Italia. Los que ya obtuvieron por derecho de sangre no pierden la ciudadanía. Se estima que al 30% de toda la nación le llega alguna gota de sangre italiana. Todas esas personas tenían derecho a la ciudadanía. Ahora hay muchísimas entidades que están poniendo objeciones“, precisó.
[...]
La gaceta oficial en la que se comunicó el cambio para acceder a la ciudadanía italiana. Gentileza: Gruppo Noi Siamo.
[...]
Precisó que esta medida del gobierno italiano no tiene un fin de exclusión, sino, se busca que las personas que buscan la ciudadanía “tengan un vínculo real con Italia”.
[...]
Por otra parte, desde la Embajada de Italia en Paraguay detallaron que en los próximos días se espera una reunión para abordar este tema como también las votaciones por el referéndum y la “Festa in Piazza”, una tradicional fiesta celebrada en la Plaza Italia de Asunción.",

        "description": "Celebrata la Giornata dell’Immigrante italiano in Paraguay - Gente d'Italia

i in Europa

Home**Italiani nel mondo**Celebrata la Giornata dell’Immigrante italiano in Paraguay
* Italiani nel mondo
# Celebrata la Giornata dell’Immigrante italiano in Paraguay
3 Marzo 2025
**13
**0
**
Condividi su Facebook
**
Tweet su Twitter
******
[...]
ASUNCION - La**Comunità di origine italiana in Paraguay**ha celebrato la**“Giornata dell’Immigrante italiano”**, come previsto dalla legge emanata dal Congresso del Paraguay nel maggio del 2019, promossa nell’ambito della conclusione del trattato bilaterale sul reciproco riconoscimento della doppia cittadinanza, firmato il 26 febbraio 2019.
[...]
L’evento si inserisce nel quadro delle iniziative previste per la valorizzazione dell’anniversario dei**150 anni di relazioni diplomatiche e commerciali tra Italia e Paraguay.**
[...]
L’atto ufficiale ha visto la partecipazione delle autorità locali, dei rappresentanti delle associazioni italiane e dell’Ambasciata d’Italia, presso Piazza Italia.
[...]
Nella sede storica del porto fluviale di Assunzione, si ètenuta l’inaugurazione di una**statua in bronzo**dedicata ai numerosi immigranti italiani arrivati in Paraguay già nella prima metà del diciannovesimo secolo, a riconoscimento dell’importante ruolo svolto dai nostri connazionali nello sviluppo socio-economico e culturale del Paese sudamericano.
[...]
Alla manifestazione erano presenti anche il Ministro del commercio e industria**Javier Jiménez**, la ministra del turismo**Angie Duarte**, e il sindaco di Assunzione**Oscar Rodríguez.**

Nel quadro dei 150 anni di relazioni bilaterali
[...]
ASUNCION, 27 febbraio 2025, 16:07
[...]
Celebrata in Paraguay la Giornata del migrante Italiano, nel quadro dell'anniversario dei 150 anni di relazioni diplomatiche e commerciali bilaterali. L'evento ufficiale, che si è tenuto ad Asuncion, ha visto la partecipazione dell'Ambasciatore Marcello Fondi, delle autorità locali e dei rappresentanti delle associazioni italiane, oltre ai ministri del Commercio e industria Javier Jiménez, del Turismo Angie Duarte, e il sindaco della capitale Oscar Rodríguez. Nella sede storica del porto fluviale si è tenuta l'inaugurazione di una statua in bronzo dedicata al rilevante numero di immigranti italiani arrivati in Paraguay già nella prima metà del diciannovesimo secolo, a riconoscimento dell'importante ruolo svolto nello sviluppo socio-economico e culturale del Paese sudamericano.",

[...]
The event is part of the initiatives planned to mark the 150th anniversary of diplomatic and commercial relations between Italy and Paraguay.
[...]

        "title": "Paraguay busca introducir el italiano en las escuelas públicas con apoyo de la Società Dante Alighieri - Última Hora | Noticias de Paraguay y el mundo, las 24 horas. Noticias nacionales e internacionales, deportes, política. Noticias de último momento.",
        "description": "Paraguay busca introducir el italiano en las escuelas públicas con apoyo de la Società Dante Alighieri - Última Hora | Noticias de Paraguay y el mundo, las 24 horas. Noticias nacionales e internacionales, deportes, política. Noticias de último momento.
[...]
introducir el italiano en las escuelas públicas con apoyo de la Società Dante Alighieri
[...]
## Durante su visita oficial a Roma, Italia, el titular del Ministerio de Educación y Ciencias (MEC), Luis Fernando Ramírez, anunció avances concretos en la posibilidad de incorporar la enseñanza del idioma italiano en las escuelas públicas del país, gracias a una colaboración con la Società Dante Alighieri.
[...]
El titular del MEC, Luis Ramírez, está en visita oficial a Roma y mantuvo una reunión con miembros de la Sociedad Dante Alighieri.
[...]
“Afianzando aspectos culturales y lingüísticos en pos de mayores oportunidades para nuestros compatriotas, unimos esfuerzos con la Società Dante Alighieri con miras a que las escuelas públicas paraguayas tengan como opción el idioma italiano”, expresó el ministro tras la reunión mantenida en el Palazzo Firenze, sede de la prestigiosa institución cultural en Roma.
[...]
Acompañado por el embajador del Paraguay en Italia, María José Argaña Mateu, Ramírez fue recibido por el secretario general de la Sociedad Dante Alighieri, Alessandro Masi, el vicesecretario Andrea Vincenzoni y el consejero central Salvatore Italia.
[...]
“Desde los clubes sociales, desde las organizaciones escolares han tenido una participación muy fructífera en el desarrollo de la sociedad. Nosotros creemos que hoy tenemos mucho que atender y necesitamos como país fortalecer la universidad. Pero ante esta crisis que hay en el mundo de la universidad y su vínculo con el trabajo, nosotros vemos que podemos hacer una muy buena síntesis con las experiencias italianas. Por eso queremos que en la escuela pública paraguaya sea una opción el italiano”, dijo el titular de la cartera educativa.
[...]
Por su parte, el secretario general Masi afirmó que “el Paraguay está en el centro de la atención de la Sociedad Dante Alighieri”, y recordó que el presidente italiano Sergio Mattarella visitó en 2023 una de las escuelas de la Dante en Asunción.
[...]
“Nuestro método, nuestras soluciones tecnológicas y nuestros productos editoriales para la enseñanza contribuirán a reforzar la presencia del italiano en Paraguay”, destacó.
[...]
Uno de los ejes de la cooperación será la capacitación docente, incluyendo la certificación Plida y el aprovechamiento de la plataforma Dante.global, que ofrece cursos tanto para estudiantes como para adultos interesados en la lengua y la cultura italianas, según informó el portal Notizie Geopolitiche, que destacó que en nuestro país actualmente el Comité Dante Alighieri de Asunción administra dos escuelas afiliadas a la red Adasim.",

## InterNations verbindet Schweizer in Paraguay
[...]
Sind Sie auf der Suche nach anderen Schweizer in Paraguay? Brauchen Sie Ratschläge und Tipps von Ihren Schweizer Landsleuten? Oder möchten Sie zusammen mit anderen Schweizer die Sehenswürdigkeiten Paraguays erkunden? Ganz egal ob Sie schon als Expat in Paraguay leben oder noch zu Hause in der Schweiz sind und Ihren Umzug ins Ausland vorbereiten, InterNations hat alles, was Sie dafür brauchen! Unsere vertrauensvolle Community hat sich seit der Gründung im Jahr 2007 schnell als internationale Networking-Plattform für Expats und Global Minds weltweit etabliert. Unter unseren Mitgliedern ist eine beträchtliche Zahl an Schweizer Expats in Paraguay, welche aus ganz der Schweiz kommen – von Bern über Genf bis hin zu Zürich.
[...]
## Nehmen Sie an aufregenden Events in Paraguay teil
[...]
Abgesuchen Sie unsere regelmäßigen Events und Aktivitäten für Expats in Paraguay, um Gleichgesinnte vor Ort zu treffen.
[...]
## Erhalten Sie Informationen und Tipps über das Leben in Paraguay von Schweizer Expats
[...]
Beim Start ins Expat-Leben in Paraguay geht es um viel mehr als nur um das Packen der Umzugskartons und die notwendigen Behördengänge. Der regelmäßige Austausch mit Schweizer Landsleuten ist ein genauso wichtiger Aspekt der Expat-Erfahrung und kann Ihnen helfen, sich schneller an die Kultur und die Leute in der neuen Heimat zu gewöhnen. InterNations hilft Ihnen, andere Schweizer in Paraguay zu treffen, die zum Beispiel in Asunción, Ciudad del Este und vielen anderen Orten im ganzen Land leben, und sich mit ihnen auszutauschen. Besuchen Sie einfach unser Netzwerk von Schweizer in Paraguay und schauen Sie sich um. Falls Sie sich mal wieder in Ihrer Muttersprache unterhalten, einfach über das letzte Spiel Ihres Lieblingsteams quatschen, oder herausfinden möchten, wo Sie Schweizer Lebensmittel finden, treten Sie der Schweizer InterNations Community bei: - Surfen Sie durch unser hochwertiges Netzwerk, um gleichgesinnte Expats aus der Schweiz in Paraguay zu finden. - Erhalten und teilen Sie Ratschläge in unseren Paraguay Expat-Foren – zum Beispiel wie man ein Kindermädchen findet, welches die Muttersprache der Kinder spricht, oder wo man Rösti in Asunción kaufen kann. - Stöbern Sie durch unseren Expat-Guide, um wichtige Informationen über das Expat-Leben in Paraguay zu erhalten. Wir verbinden Sie mit hilfsbereiten und freundlichen Schweizer Expats in Paraguay!
[...]
## Nehmen Sie an verschiedenen Aktivitäten teil, um Schweizer in Paraguay zu treffen
[...]
Sie sind außerdem eingeladen, an unseren regelmäßigen Expat-Events und Aktivitäten in Paraguay teilzunehmen. Bei unseren Events ist für jeden Geschmack etwas dabei: Vom gemeinsamen Abendessen um Sopa Paraguaya zu genießen, bis hin zu großen After-Work-Events mit hunderten Gästen in den besten Locations. Sie können sich auch einem unserer Expat-Ausflüge anschließen, um zum Beispiel imposante Sehenswürdigkeiten in Paraguay zu entdecken. Unsere Events und Aktivitäten sind eine gute Möglichkeit, andere Expats aus der Schweiz und der ganzen Welt in einer lockeren Atmosphäre zu treffen. Sind Sie interessiert? Dann melden Sie sich für eine Mitgliedschaft an und schon bald werden Sie mit einem freundlichen Grüezi, salut, buongiorno, bainvegni von unseren Schweizer in Paraguay begrüßt.
[...]

19 mayo, 2025, 16:17 - Por Jhonny Garay "Por --
[...]
La nueva ley en España permite a paraguayos sin contrato acceder a la residencia si prueban solvencia y arraigo social tras 3 años en el país.
[...]
A partir de este martes 20 de mayo, entra en vigencia una nueva normativa migratoria en España que representa una oportunidad clave para miles de paraguayos que residen en ese país en situación irregular. La reforma facilita la obtención de la residencia legal por arraigo social, incluso sin contar con un contrato laboral.
[...]
El cambio beneficiaría a más de 30.000 compatriotas, que hasta ahora enfrentaban trabas para acceder a la documentación por la exigencia de empleo formal. A partir de ahora, si una persona lleva al menos 3 años viviendo en España, podrá iniciar el trámite de regularización demostrando solvencia económica mínima de 7.200 euros (aproximadamente Gs. 65 millones), además de un informe que acredite su integración social.
[...]
Haber vivido al menos 3 años en España de forma continua.
[...]
Demostrar solvencia económica (ahorros, cuentas bancarias, propiedades u otros ingresos lícitos).
[...]
Presentar un informe del ayuntamiento donde conste tu integración (idioma, cultura, vínculos sociales).
[...]
Quienes no cuenten con solvencia, podrán optar por otra vía: el contrato laboral. Ahora, ya no es necesario que sea de jornada completa. Un contrato de medio tiempo también es válido, según explicó la abogada paraguaya radicada en Madrid, Josefina Orellana.
[...]
La reforma también incorpora una modalidad llamada “arraigo de segunda oportunidad”, destinada a personas que ya contaron con permiso de residencia, pero lo perdieron por vencimiento o incumplimientos. Estos casos no deberán empezar de cero, sino solo demostrar que han residido de forma continuada en el país.
[...]
Un punto innovador de la reforma es la extensión de derechos de reagrupación familiar. Si tenés un familiar cercano con residencia legal en España, podrás solicitar vivir con él, incluso si no están casados o reconocidos como pareja de hecho. Esta medida abarca a:
[...]
Padres de niños españoles, quienes podrán obtener residencia directamente sin esperar los dos años.
[...]
Con la entrada en vigencia de esta reforma, se prevé una avalancha de solicitudes, lo que podría retrasar los trámites, más aún considerando que se aproxima la temporada de vacaciones en las oficinas de extranjería.
[...]
Esta nueva ley abre una vía más accesible y humana para la regularización de miles de personas, incluyendo a una gran cantidad de paraguayos que han formado vida en España y que ahora tendrán una chance concreta de salir de la irregularidad.",

        "url": "https://www.exteriores.gob.es/Embajadas/asuncion/es/ViajarA/Paginas/Establecerse-en-Paraguay.aspx",
        "title": "Establecerse en Paraguay",
        "description": "Información sobre el traslado de residencia de ciudadanos españoles a Paraguay.
[...]
El organismo paraguayo competente es la Dirección Nacional de Migraciones (http://www.migraciones.gov.py/)
[...]
Antes de realizar un viaje a Paraguay se recomienda informarse ante las autoridades competentes:
[...]
Embajada de Paraguay en España: http://www.embajadadelparaguay.es/
[...]
Si tiene intención de establecerse en Paraguay, se recomienda recabar y apostillar los documentos personales que pueda necesitar antes de salir de España (certificado literal de nacimiento y matrimonio, títulos académicos, sentencias de incapacitación, divorcio, guarda y custodia, patria potestad, etc.).
[...]
Desde que Paraguay se adhirió al Convenio de la Haya en septiembre de 2014, las legalizaciones consulares han sido suprimidas. En su lugar, los documentos que deban surtir efectos ante el Estado español deben ser debidamente apostillados por el Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores.
[...]
Los enlaces que se ofrecen a continuación corresponden a la Dirección Nacional de Migraciones: se puede consultar la normativa legal vigente desde 2022 (Ley Nº 6984 de Migraciones), los diversos trámites para solicitar la residencia en Paraguay, los requisitos ​documentales y el procedimiento para su tramitación:
[...]
- Ley Nº 6984 de Migraciones: https://www.migraciones.gov.py/application/files/6016/6748/3287/Ley_6984_de_Migraciones_-_2022.pdf
- Trámites de Residencia en el Paraguay: https://www.migraciones.gov.py/index.php/tramites/residencia",

        "description": "## El embajador paraguayo en España, Justo Apodaca, informó que hace tres días el Gobierno de España aprobó un nuevo reglamento de Extranjería que podría beneficiar a numerosos paraguayos que viven de manera ilegal en ese país. ## 1) El nuevo convenio entre España y Paraguay
[...]
En 2025 España y Paraguay firmaron un acuerdo de migración laboral circular para facilitar la contratación en origen de personas trabajadoras paraguayas. Este marco garantiza procesos oficiales de selección, contratos temporales bajo la normativa española, alta en la Seguridad Social y retorno seguro al finalizar.
[...]
## 2) Contratos por cuenta ajena y ocupaciones de difícil cobertura
[...]
### Contratación en origen (por cuenta ajena)
[...]
- Recursos
- Residencia inicial y trabajo vinculados a un contrato previo al viaje.
- Selección gestionada entre el Ministerio de Trabajo de Paraguay y autoridades españolas.
- Derechos laborales, cotización y cobertura sanitaria desde el primer día.
[...]
### Ocupaciones de difícil cobertura
[...]
España actualiza trimestralmente el Catálogo de Ocupaciones de Difícil Cobertura, donde hay demanda que no puede cubrirse con mano de obra local.
[...]
- Agricultura y campañas de temporada.
- Hostelería y restauración en zonas turísticas.
- Construcción y oficios especializados.
- Instalación eléctrica y perfiles técnicos.
- Sector marítimo y pesquero.
[...]
## 3) Arraigo como alternativa
[...]
Si ya te encuentras en España, el Reglamento de Extranjería contempla vías de regularización por arraigo:
[...]
- Arraigo social: 2 años en España + vínculos o contrato (RD 1155/2024).
- Arraigo laboral: 2 años de estancia + relación laboral acreditada.
- Arraigo familiar / para la formación: según circunstancias del caso.
[...]
## 4) Trámites principales
[...]
1. Selección en origen (convenio) y firma del contrato por cuenta ajena.
2. Visado en el Consulado de España en Asunción.
3. Apostilla de La Haya (actas, antecedentes y títulos si aplican).
4. Llegada a España: empadronamiento, NIE y TIE, alta en Seguridad Social, sanidad y escolarización de menores.
[...]
### Convenio España–Paraguay
[...]
Actualización normativa y requisitos prácticos para aplicar al programa de migración laboral.
[...]
### Cuenta ajena y difícil cobertura

[...]
Obtener un permiso de residencia en España para paraguayos es un proceso que abre las puertas a nuevas oportunidades en Europa. Con un creciente interés en la vida y el trabajo en este vibrante país, muchos paraguayos están buscando establecerse en España. Este artículo ofrece una guía clara y práctica sobre los requisitos, procedimientos y beneficios de obtener este permiso, facilitando así la transición hacia una nueva vida en el continente europeo.
[...]
Los paraguayos necesitan pasaporte válido, comprobante de medios económicos, seguro médico y antecedentes penales para solicitar el permiso de residencia en España.
[...]
- Acceso a servicios sociales: Con el permiso de residencia, los paraguayos pueden acceder a servicios de salud, educación y otros beneficios sociales que ofrece el sistema español, mejorando así su calidad de vida.
[...]
Obtener la residencia en España requiere un compromiso a largo plazo, ya que es necesario residir de manera legal y continua durante diez años antes de presentar la solicitud. Este periodo asegura que los solicitantes hayan integrado adecuadamente en la sociedad española y cumplan con todos los requisitos legales establecidos.
[...]
Los paraguayos que deseen visitar España tienen un acceso relativamente fácil, ya que pueden permanecer hasta 90 días solo presentando su pasaporte al llegar. Este acuerdo simplifica considerablemente el proceso de entrada, habilitando que los viajeros disfruten de su estancia sin complicaciones burocráticas.
[...]
Sin prohibición, a partir de la última parte de 2022, se implementa un nuevo requisito: el permiso de viaje ETIAS. Este permiso es necesario para aquellos que planean visitar los países del espacio Schengen, lo que incluye a España. Por lo tanto, es fundamental asegurarse de obtener este permiso antes de viajar, garantizando así una experiencia sin contratiempos al explorar la rica cultura y paisajes de España.
[...]
Para lograr una residencia exitosa en España, es fundamental seguir una serie de pasos clave que faciliten el proceso. Primero, investiga las diferentes opciones de visado disponibles, ya sea por trabajo, estudio o reagrupación familiar, y elige la que mejor se adapte a tus necesidades. Luego, asegúrate de reunir toda la documentación necesaria, como pasaporte, certificados y pruebas de solvencia económica. Una vez que tengas todo listo, presenta tu solicitud en la embajada o consulado correspondiente, siguiendo las pautas establecidas. Finalmente, mantente informado sobre el estado de tu solicitud y aprovecha las oportunidades de integración cultural y social que ofrece el país, lo que te permitirá adaptarte más fácilmente y disfrutar de tu nueva vida en España.
[...]

Este proceso puede variar notoriamente de un lugar a otro, por lo que es fundamental informarse sobre los requisitos específicos de la nación donde planeas residir. Asegúrate de recopilar toda la documentación necesaria, como certificados de nacimiento, antecedentes penales y pruebas de medios económicos, ya que estos documentos son esenciales para facilitar tu solicitud.
[...]

Recuerda que contar con la asesoría de expertos puede hacer la diferencia y garantizar que tu trámite sea ágil y exitoso.

La regularización incluye también a los solicitantes de asilo que hayan presentado su solicitud antes de 2026. Esta población se había declarado afectada tras la última reforma migratoria, que no los consideró para la agilización de los plazos.",

      {
        "url": "https://legalfournier.com/en/spain-residency/paraguay/",
        "title": "Residency from Paraguay to Spain | Legal Fournier",
        "description": "# Paraguay to Spain: Complete Residency Guide
[...]
Moving from Paraguay: Complete Residency Guide to Spain. Visa requirements. Legal Fournier.
[...]
## Moving to Spain from Paraguay: Complete 2026 Guide
[...]
Over 106,000 Paraguayans live in Spain-a 53-fold increase in just 20 years. Visa-free entry, the 2-year citizenship path, and a brand new tax treaty (effective January 2026) make Spain increasingly attractive.
[...]
- Visa: Not required (90 days visa-free)
- Citizenship: 2 years (Ibero-American)
- Dual Nationality: Allowed
- Tax Treaty: Active since January 2026 (NEW)
[...]
The advantages compound: visa-free entry for exploration, shared language for integration, 2-year citizenship path for permanent settlement, and now a new double taxation treaty effective January 2026-the first treaty Paraguay has signed with an OECD country.
[...]
## Visa Options
[...]
Digital Nomad. Remote work,
[...]
2,763/month
[...]
Paraguayans can enter Spain visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. To stay longer, you must obtain a residence visa. Note: ETIAS authorization (€7) will be required starting late 2026.
[...]
## Citizenship in 2 Years
[...]
As an Ibero-American nation, Paraguay qualifies for Spains accelerated citizenship. After 2 years of continuous legal residence, you can apply for Spanish nationality.
[...]
- 2 years continuous legal residence (absences under 3 months)
- CCSE exam (Spanish constitutional and cultural knowledge)
- DELE A2 exam exemption-Paraguayans are exempt from language test
- Clean criminal record in Paraguay and Spain
- Proof of economic means and social integration
[...]
## Spanish Consulates in Paraguay
[...]
Spain has additional representations in Paraguay beyond the main embassy. Contact the embassy for the nearest location.
[...]
## Required Documents
[...]
#### Prepare for your application:
[...]
- Passport valid for at least 6 months
- Criminal record from Paraguay, apostilled
- Medical certificate from authorized physician
- Proof of income (bank statements, contracts)
- Health insurance from Spanish-authorized provider
- Application form completed and signed
- Photos passport size, white background
[...]
All Paraguayan documents must be apostilled.
[...]
## Tax Considerations
[...]
### Paraguay-Spain Double Tax Treaty (NEW. 2026)
[...]
Signed March 2023, in force October 2024, effective January 1, 2026. This is Paraguays first double taxation treaty with an OECD country.
[...]
183-Day Rule: If you spend more than 183 days in Spain, you become a Spanish tax resident. The new treaty provides clear rules for avoiding double taxation.
[...]
Can I enter Spain without a visa?
[...]
Yes. Paraguayans can enter Spain visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. For longer stays, you need a residence visa.
[...]
The treaty effective January 2026 prevents double taxation and provides reduced withholding rates on dividends and other income. Its particularly beneficial for business owners and investors.
[...]
is illegal. Get the correct visa before starting any employment in Spain.
[...]
. Income requirements
[...]
members also qualify for
[...]
2-year citizenship
[...]
At Legal Fournier, we manage everything. Residence applications prepared correctly. Tax planning under the new treaty. NIE registration, empadronamiento, social security, and citizenship application support at the 2-year mark.",

      }

Nosso serviço mais procurado: já ajudamos mais de 700 pessoas de mais de 40 países a obter residência no Paraguai, e mal podemos esperar para ajudar você também.

[...]
## Residência no Paraguai em questão de semanas
[...]
Tornar-se residente no Paraguai não precisa ser caro e complicado. Explicamos o processo claramente, o guiamos em cada etapa do caminho e podemos oferecer suporte em inglês, espanhol, português e italiano.
[...]
Para todo o nosso serviço, você pagará uma única taxa, e somente depois de ter realmente iniciado os procedimentos. Nossos pacotes para residência temporária e permanente incluem transfer do aeroporto, suporte ilimitado, traduções, transporte entre escritórios públicos e cobrem todos os custos diretos dos processos burocráticos.
[...]
Não. O processo para obter residência no Paraguai mudou no final de 2022. Hoje, não é mais necessário depositar nenhum valor em um banco.

única viagem?
[...]
Após as mudanças que ocorreram em setembro de 2024, agora podemos novamente concluir a solicitação de residência e da cédula em uma única viagem curta ao Paraguai.
[...]
Quanto tempo leva para concluir o processo?

Com o Pacote Prata, você receberá o cartão de residência e a carteira de identidade em 120 dias. Com o Pacote Ouro, em 50-55 dias.

Não, o Paraguai não tem um requisito de solvência econômica para conceder residência. Isso se aplica tanto à residência temporária quanto à permanente.",

      {
        "url": "https://paraguaypathways.com/pt-br/programa-de-residencia-do-paraguai-como-funciona-em-2025/",
        "title": "Programa de residência do Paraguai: como funciona em 2025",
        "description": "Programa de residência do Paraguai: como funciona em 2025
[...]
O Paraguai se destaca como um dos países mais fáceis e convenientes para obter uma segunda residência. Além disso, seu sistema tributário favorável o torna um destino popular para aqueles que buscam uma residência secundária, não apenas para si, mas também para seus negócios. As leis que regulamentam o processo de residência no Paraguai passaram por mudanças significativas, notadamente em 2020 e novamente em 2022, introduzindo várias atualizações sobre os pré-requisitos e etapas para obter residência. Este guia já está atualizado para refletir todas essas mudanças, incluindo as que ocorreram após setembro de 2024 e que dizem respeito especificamente ao documento de identidade paraguaio.
[...]
existem duas opções principais:
[...]
- Residência temporária é válida por 2 anos, com possibilidade de renovação e conversão em residência permanente.
- Residência permanente concede residência indefinida no país e requer renovação a cada 10 anos.
[...]
Os direitos que você obtém com ambas as opções são exatamente os mesmos. As únicas distinções entre os dois tipos de residência são seus períodos de validade e os requisitos. Atualmente, a rota mais simples e econômica é primeiro obter a residência temporária e depois convertê-la em residência permanente.
[...]
- Na chegada ao país, é necessário passar por uma série de órgãos (Polícia, Interpol, Departamento de TI, Ministério das Relações Exteriores e Departamento de Migrações) para obter o carnet de radicación temporal. Este documento, comumente referido como “cartão de residência”, leva em média 95-105 dias para ser emitido em 2025. Trabalhando com uma agência especializada, você pode reduzir esse tempo para 35-45 dias.
[...]
- Depois do carnet de radicación temporal, pode-se dirigir ao Departamento de Identificação da Polícia Paraguaia para obter o documento de identidade (cédula). Desde setembro de 2024, tornou-se estritamente necessário solicitar o novo formato do documento de identidade. Este é o formato que incorpora o microchip e só pode ser obtido através de impressões digitais eletrônicas e assinatura digital.
[...]
A residência permanente concede o direito de residir indefinidamente no Paraguai, com a condição de renovação a cada 10 anos.
[...]
1. O método mais direto é primeiro obter a residência temporária e depois convertê-la em permanente. Você pode mudar sua residência temporária para permanente após 21 meses. Neste caso, você precisará mostrar uma prova muito básica de solvência. Para saber mais sobre o processo, confira nosso guia sobre conversão de residência temporária em permanente.
[...]
2. Alternativamente, pode-se obter diretamente residência permanente por investimento. Isso requer o compromisso de investir pelo menos 70.000$ no país, apresentando um plano de negócios detalhado que mostre como o dinheiro será investido. Para obter residência permanente desta forma, é necessário apenas mostrar uma conta bancária com mais de 70.000$ em fundos, então o candidato tem 6 meses para executar seu plano de negócios.
[...]
Um dos principais benefícios do programa de residência do
[...]
- Evitar fazer uma ausência injustificada do país de 12 meses consecutivos durante a residência temporária;
- Evitar fazer uma ausência injustificada do país de 36 meses consecutivos durante a residência permanente.
[...]
A partir do início de 2025, há discussões sobre mudar a lei e oficialmente remover o requisito de visitas obrigatórias. Dito isso, até que a lei seja oficialmente alterada, recomendamos que nossos clientes continuem cumprindo com as visitas obrigatórias. Uma visita pode ser teoricamente de apenas um minuto, já que não há exigência de permanência por um tempo específico em cada ocasião que você visita o país.",
        "position": 2
      },
      {
        "url": "https://www.gov.pt/servicos/obter-autorizacao-de-residencia-cplp",
        "title": "Obter Autorização de Residência CPLP - gov.pt",
        "description": "# Obter Autorização de Residência CPLP
[...]
Pessoas nacionais da Comunidade de Países de Língua Portuguesa (CPLP) podem obter a autorização de residência em Portugal, online e em 72 horas. Pode obter esta autorização de residência usando a sua manifestação de interesse ou visto consular.
[...]
Para obter a autorização de residência CPLP online não precisa de se deslocar a um balcão do Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras (SEF), a não ser que haja menores de idade envolvidos.
[...]
Nota: Se obtiver a autorização de residência CPLP através da manifestação de interesse, a manifestação de interesse que apresentou irá perder o efeito. Isso é normal e significa que já não vai precisar mais da manifestação de interesse.
[...]
Pessoas de cidadania de um país membro da CPLP numa das seguintes situações:
[...]
- Tenham apresentado uma manifestação de interesse junto do SEF até 31 de dezembro de 2022
- Sejam titulares de visto de residência CPLP emitido a partir de 31 de outubro de 2022.
[...]
Escolha se quer solicitar a autorização de residência com manifestação de interesse ou com visto consular 

Para solicitar a autorização de residência com manifestação de interesse, vai ter de se autenticar com os dados de acesso ao portal SAPA do SEF(através do qual submeteu a sua manifestação de interesse)
[...]
Para solicitar a autorização de residência com visto consular, vai ter de criar um registo novo de utilizador. Irá receber um e-mail para confirmar o registo.
[...]
Atenção: só pode fazer o pagamento 48 horas depois de gerar o DUC. O SEF envia um e-mail a confirmar que o pagamento foi feito.
[...]
1. Guarde ou imprima o documento único de cobrança (DUC) para fazer o pagamento.
2. Depois de receber o e-mail de confirmação de pagamento, aceda novamente ao portal para obter a autorização de residência CPLP
3. No canto superior direito do ecrã, carregue em “aceder a autorização de residência CPLP”
4. Autentique-se com o seu e-mail e palavra-passe para poder emitir o certificado de autorização de residência CPLP.
[...]
Para obter a autorização de residência CPLP online é preciso uma das duas seguintes condições:
[...]
- ter submetido, através do portal SAPA do SEF, uma manifestação de interesse até 31 de dezembro de 2022
- ser titular de visto consular de residência CPLP emitido a partir de 31 de outubro de 2022.
[...]
Se tem visto consular de residência, vai precisar de anexar o seu documento de identificação válido, por exemplo o passaporte, em formato digital (JPEG ou PDF).
[...]
Se apresentou uma manifestação de interesse, confirme se o documento de identificação associado à manifestação de interesse está válido.
[...]
Se houver menores de idade envolvidos com a autorização de residência CPLP online, é necessário deslocar-se depois a um balcão do SEF para regularizar a situação.
[...]
A autorização de residência CPLP é válida por um ano. Pode ser renovada duas vezes por períodos de dois anos. A autorização tem uma validade máxima total de cinco anos. Após cinco anos de residência temporária em Portugal, pode pedir a autorização de residência permanente.
[...]
A emissão da autorização de residência CPLP custa 15 euros.
[...]
Ao solicitar a autorização de residência, é criado um documento único de cobrança (DUC) com as indicações para fazer o pagamento.
[...]
Atenção: só pode fazer o pagamento 48 horas depois de gerar o DUC. O SEF envia um e-mail a confirmar que o pagamento foi feito.
[...]
a Mobilidade entre os Estados-Membros da Comunidade dos Países de Língua Portuguesa (C
[...]
inado em Luanda,
[...]
de julho de 2021
[...]
