- Outros documentos
- Estrangeiros em Portugal
- Migrantes em Portugal
[...]
O serviço "Obter Autorização de Residência CPLP" é da responsabilidade da entidade Agência para a Integração, Migrações e Asilo.",
        "position": 3
      },
      {
        "url": "https://paraguaysovereign.com/residency/vs-portugal/",
        "title": "Paraguay vs Portugal Residency 2026 | Which is Better? | Paraguay Sovereign",
        "description": "| Factor | Paraguay | Portugal |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Residency Cost | $2,290-4,495 | €500,000+ |
| Investment Required | None | €500,000 fund |
| Timeline | 60-90 days | 12-24 months |
| Minimum Stay | Once per year | 7 days/year |
| Path to Citizenship | 3 years | 5 years + A2 language |
| Foreign Income Tax | Territorial system* | 0% for 5 years, then 20-28%* |
| Local Income Tax | 10% flat | Progressive scale |
| Fund Lock-up | N/A | 6-10 years |
| Crypto-Friendly | Yes (unregulated) | No (heavily regulated) |
| Monthly Living Cost | $1,500-2,500 | €2,500-4,000 |
[...]
- Real estate removed: No longer an option (residential and commercial excluded)
- Fund-only: Must invest €500,000 in qualified venture capital funds
- Longer wait times: Processing now takes 12-24 months due to AIMA backlog
- Fund lock-up: Your capital is typically locked for 6-10 years
[...]
upfront cost difference is obvious:
[...]
But there is another cost most people forget: opportunity cost
[...]
### Portugal: 5-Year Holiday (Source: Financas.gov.pt)
[...]
- • Foreign income: 0% for first 5 years
- • After 5 years: 20-28% on foreign income*
- • Wealth tax: may apply
[...]
- • More complex tax code
[...]
- • Holiday is a one-time benefit
- • Must maintain residency to qualify
[...]
Until 2024, the NHR program offered 0% foreign income tax for 10 years. New applicants now get only 5 years. After that, foreign income is taxed at Portugal standard rates (20-28% depending on income type). Paraguay's advantage is not a temporary holiday but a territorial framework, subject to source characterization and compliance.
[...]
| Factor | Paraguay | Portugal |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Residency to Citizenship | 3 years (PR) | 5 years residency |
| Language Requirement | Spanish (basic) | A2 Portuguese required |
| Passport Strength | Developing nation | EU passport |
| Total Timeline | ~5 years | ~7 years |
[...]
- €500,000+ investment required
- 6-10 year fund lock-up period
- Temporary tax benefits (5 years)
- Slower processing (12-24 months)
- Higher cost of living
- Not crypto-friendly
- €200,000+ in opportunity cost
[...]
For most people seeking residency, Paraguay is the rational choice. You get a lower-cost territorial-tax route, zero investment requirement, faster processing, and unmatched flexibility. Portugal makes sense only if an EU passport is your non-negotiable end goal and you have €500,000 you do not need access to for 6-10 years.
[...]
Yes, but the program changed significantly in 2023. Real estate investment was removed. Now the main option is €500,000 in qualified venture capital funds, with a 6-10 year lock-up period. Processing times have also increased to 12-24 months.
[...]
Paraguay uses a territorial system, so treatment depends on whether income is genuinely foreign-sourced and properly documented. Portugal's NHR program provides temporary benefits for 5 years, after which normal Portuguese tax treatment applies. For long-term planning, Paraguay is usually the cleaner option if your income is genuinely foreign-sourced.
[...]
Paraguay processes in 60-90 days. Portugal Golden Visa currently takes 12-24 months due to AIMA backlog. Paraguay is significantly faster, with legal status beginning immediately upon application filing.
[...]
The Non-Habitual Resident (NHR) program offers tax benefits to new residents. Until 2024, it provided 0% foreign income tax for 10 years. This was reduced to 5 years for new applicants. After NHR expires, foreign income is taxed at Portugal's standard rates (20-28% depending on income type).
[...]
After 5 years of NHR benefits, foreign income (dividends, interest, pensions) is taxed at 20-28%. The benefit cannot be renewed. Many expats then consider relocating to a territorial-tax jurisdiction like Paraguay, though this requires starting a new residency process.",
        "position": 4
      },
      {
        "url": "https://meuplanopy.com.br/residencia",
        "title": "Residência no Paraguai | Meu Plano Py",
        "description": "# Residência Temporária instituída pela Lei nº
[...]
.984/2022 sobre Migração
[...]
A Residência Temporária no Paraguai, instituída pela Lei nº 6.984/2022, é uma autorização concedida pela Direção Nacional de Migração a estrangeiros que desejam se estabelecer no país para atividades lícitas. Possui validade de até dois anos, prorrogável, e é pré-requisito para a Residência Permanente. Seus benefícios incluem o status de "residente precário" (permitindo sair/reentrar, estudar e trabalhar) e acesso à Carteira de Identidade Paraguaia.
[...]
Os requisitos gerais incluem documentos de identidade, visto consular (se aplicável), certificado de entrada no país, certidões de nascimento e estado civil, e certificados de antecedentes criminais (do país de origem, de residências anteriores e da INTERPOL, além de um específico para estrangeiros emitido pela Polícia Nacional). Menores de 18 anos exigem documentos adicionais dos pais ou responsáveis e, em alguns casos, procuração especial. O processo envolve o pagamento de taxas e a apresentação de declarações juramentadas. Documentos em língua estrangeira devem ser traduzidos e legalizados/apostilados, exceto os emitidos no Brasil em português. O processo é pessoal e presencial, e a Direção Nacional de Imigração pode solicitar documentos adicionais.
[...]
Residência Temporária no MERCOSUL no Paraguai, destinada a cidadãos de países signatários (Argentina, Brasil, Uruguai, Bolívia, Chile, Peru, Colômbia e Equador). Esta residência tem validade de dois anos, improrrogáveis, e permite que o estrangeiro resida temporariamente para exercer atividades lícitas. Os requisitos são similares aos da Residência Temporária geral, incluindo documentos de identidade, certidões de nascimento e estado civil, e antecedentes criminais. Há requisitos adicionais para menores de 18 anos. O processo é pessoal e presencial, com taxas específicas.
[...]
Residência Permanente no Paraguai para estrangeiros que já possuem a Residência Temporária instituída pela Lei nº 6.984/2022. Esta autorização permite residir por tempo indeterminado, com renovação do cartão a cada 10 anos e acesso à Carteira de Identidade Paraguaia. A solicitação de mudança de categoria deve ser feita até 3 meses antes do vencimento da temporária, com prazos e multas para atrasos. Os requisitos incluem documentos de identidade, comprovante da residência temporária, antecedentes criminais (Estrangeiros, Boa Conduta, Criminais e INTERPOL), e comprovação de solvência financeira, com opções detalhadas para diversas categorias (profissionais, técnicos, funcionários, comerciantes, acionistas, agricultores, religiosos, aposentados, dependentes e estudantes). Há requisitos adicionais para menores de 18 anos, e as observações sobre tradução e legalização/apostilamento de documentos são mantidas.
[...]
Requisitos: A Residência Permanente exige, além de muitos documentos já solicitados na temporária, o comprovante da Residência Temporária anterior, Certificado de Boa Conduta, e, principalmente, a comprovação de solvência financeira, que é detalhada extensivamente no site https://migraciones.gov.py/ e não é um requisito no primeiro texto para a residência temporária.
[...]
## Requisitos
[...]
Original + cópia autenticada de certificado de antecedentes da INTERPOL (a partir dos 14 anos)
[...]
Original + cópia autenticada do certificado de antecedentes para estrangeiros, emitido pelo Departamento de Informática da Polícia Nacional (a partir de 14 anos)
[...]
Declaração juramentada perante a Direção Nacional de Migrações de compromisso de cumprimento e respeito à Constituição, leis e normativas paraguaias (emitida no momento da solicitação)
[...]
Declaração juramentada perante a Direção Nacional de Migrações sobre a atividade profissional/ocupação que realizará e o domicílio no país (emitida no momento da solicitação)",
        "position": 5
      },
      {
        "url": "https://destination-paraguay.com/pt/residencia-permanente-no-paraguai-guia-100-gratuito-2026/",
        "title": "Residência Permanente no Paraguai: Guia 100% Gratuito (2026)",
        "description": "O essencial a reter: A residência permanente no Paraguai exige agora dois anos de residência temporária (lei 6984/2022). Duas opções: via padrão ou SUACE (investimento de 70 000 USD escalonado ao longo de 10 anos). Estas regras atualizadas evitam trâmites obsoletos e garantem o seu projeto de instalação com um procedimento mais estruturado.
[...]
Em 2025, os candidatos terão duas opções principais: a via padrão, que exige uma residência temporária de dois anos, e a via de investimento através do programa SUACE. A primeira exige documentos como passaporte válido, certidões autenticadas (civilidade, casamento, etc.), comprovantes de solvência (extrato bancário ou diploma universitário) e certidões negativas de antecedentes criminais emitidas pelo país de origem e pela Interpol. Em seguida, ela permite o acesso a direitos concretos: abertura de conta bancária local, acesso à saúde pública ou à educação.
[...]

pela residência tempor
[...]
O programa SUACE (Sistema Unificado de Apertura y Cierre de Empresas) oferece uma alternativa rápida para obter a residência permanente no Paraguai a partir de 2025. Ao contrário do processo padrão, ele permite contornar os dois anos de residência temporária obrigatória. Essa via se destina principalmente a empresários e investidores que desejam se estabelecer de forma duradoura no país.
[...]
Para ter direito a ele, é necessário apresentar um projeto de investimento no valor mínimo de US$ 70.000. Esse valor não é exigido de uma só vez: um plano de negócios sólido deve detalhar sua implantação gradual ao longo de um período de 10 anos (cerca de US$ 7.000 por ano). Essa flexibilidade facilita o acesso ao programa, desde que a empresa criada contribua para a economia local.

# Residência no Paraguai: como morar no Paraguai legalmente
[...]
Esta página explica como funciona a residência no Paraguai, quais são os tipos de residência disponíveis para estrangeiros e quais etapas são necessárias para morar no Paraguai legalmente. O objetivo é ajudar você a entender o sistema antes de iniciar o processo.
[...]
O Paraguai oferece diferentes caminhos legais de residência para estrangeiros. O mais comum é a residência temporária, que pode posteriormente levar à residência permanente. Também existe a opção de residência permanente direta para investidores, em condições específicas.
[...]
- A residência é administrada pela Direção Geral de Migrações do Paraguai.
- A maioria dos procedimentos exige pelo menos uma presença física no Paraguai.
- Uma vez concedida, a residência é independente da nacionalidade.
- Ter residência não significa automaticamente ser residente fiscal.
[...]
A residência temporária é o ponto de entrada padrão para a maioria das pessoas que desejam morar no Paraguai. Ela é válida por dois anos e permite viver legalmente no país.
[...]
ência permanente.
[...]
A residência permanente é um status migratório de longo prazo. Normalmente é solicitada após a residência temporária, embora alguns perfis de investidor possam acessar diretamente esse status.
[...]
O Paraguai oferece um regime específico de residência permanente para investidores por meio do sistema SUACE. Essa opção é destinada a quem cria uma empresa ou realiza um investimento qualificado.
[...]
- Acesso direto à residência permanente.
- Vinculada a uma empresa ou investimento registrado.
- Exige comprovação de capital e atividade econômica.
- Maior volume de documentação e exigências formais.
[...]
## Documentos necessários para residência no
[...]

[...]
- Passaporte válido.
- Certidão de nascimento apostilada.
- Certidão de antecedentes criminais apostilada.
- Documentos de estado civil apostilados, se aplicável.
- Antecedentes locais emitidos no Paraguai.
[...]
Muitas nacionalidades podem entrar no Paraguai sem visto prévio para estadias curtas. Algumas nacionalidades exigem visto antes da chegada.
[...]
| Regra geral |
| --- | --- |
| Europa | Entrada sem visto para a maioria |
| América do Norte | Entrada sem visto |
| América do Sul | Entrada sem visto ou
[...]
osul |

[...]
As opções de acompanhamento, prazos e coordenação estão detalhadas na página de Serviços.
[...]
Para estadias curtas, muitas nacionalidades conseguem entrar e permanecer por um período limitado conforme as regras migratórias do passaporte. Porém, para morar no Paraguai legalmente (mudança real, rotinas, vida no país), o caminho correto costuma ser obter residência no Paraguai (geralmente começando pela residência temporária).
[...]
Na prática, a residência no Paraguai pode levar algumas semanas a alguns meses, dependendo do volume de trabalho da imigração, da organização do dossiê e da sequência correta das etapas. Um dossiê “limpo” (documentos corretos e atualizados) reduz atrasos.
[...]
Em geral, o processo pede passaporte válido, certidão de nascimento apostilada, antecedentes criminais apostilados e, quando aplicável, documentos de estado civil apostilados. Também podem existir verificações locais realizadas no Paraguai.
[...]
O ideal é começar pela preparação: checklist de documentos, revisão do dossiê e um cronograma realista para Asunción. As opções de acompanhamento e coordenação estão detalhadas na nossa página de serviços.

The National Directorate of Migration recorded a record number of residency applications in 2025, with a strong presence of Brazilians, Argentines and Europeans and a 63% jump.
[...]
Paraguay closed 2025 with a historic record for residency applications. According to the National Directorate of Migration, 47,687 applications were filed, representing growth of more than 63% compared with the previous year. The head of the institution, Jorge Kronawetter, presented these figures at a press conference and stressed that the increase consolidates an upward trend in the arrival of foreigners who choose the country as a place of residence.
[...]
Of the total applications, 34,875 corresponded to temporary residence permits, which are the initial step before obtaining permanent residence. Kronawetter explained that people who enter the country and submit the required documentation are first admitted under this transitional regime. He pointed out that, if they wish, after two years they may request an extension for an additional two-year period. This framework allows the migration authority to assess the activities they carry out and how they contribute to the country before deciding on permanent residence.
[...]

[...]

This represents a 79% increase compared with the same period of the previous year. Paraguay se fortalece como atractivo para los extranjeros por su estabilidad, crecimiento económico y previsibilidad para producir y trabajar. El 2025 se registraron 47.687 solicitudes de residencia, 63% más que en el 2024. Encabezan las solicitudes brasileños, argentinos, alemanes, bolivianos y españoles.
[...]
Según datos oficiales de la Dirección Nacional de Migraciones (DNM), actualizados al 31 de diciembre de 2025, se registraron 47.687 solicitudes de residencia, lo que representa un incremento del 63 % en comparación con el 2024. El ranking de los diez países con más radicados en 2025 se completa con Venezuela (847), Países Bajos (772), Estados Unidos (736), Rusia (509) y Francia (492), evidenciando una diversidad de orígenes y una creciente presencia tanto regional como extrarregional.
[...]
En cuanto al tipo de residencias solicitadas, durante el 2025 se ingresaron 34.875 solicitudes de residencia temporaria, de las cuales 29.976 fueron otorgadas. Por otro lado, se solicitaron 12.812 residencias permanentes, y 10.624 fueron finalmente concedidas, lo que muestra una alta tasa de aprobación en ambos regímenes.
[...]
El crecimiento sostenido en la cantidad de solicitudes y residencias otorgadas durante el 2025 da cuenta de una mayor dinámica migratoria hacia el país, así como de una demanda creciente por los procesos de regularización, tanto para residencias temporarias como permanentes, según los registros oficiales disponibles al cierre del año.",

        "url": "https://paraguaysovereign.com/residency/data/",
        "title": "Paraguay Residency Data Center 2026: Official Statistics & Trends | Paraguay Sovereign",
        "description": "This data center exists because Paraguay residency is changing faster than most information online reflects. The 2025 figures — 47,687 applications and 40,600 permits granted — are record highs by a wide margin, and the early 2026 signal suggests the growth trajectory has not plateaued. Anyone evaluating Paraguay residency today is working with outdated assumptions unless they account for this acceleration.
[...]
The core dataset spans seven years: 2019 through 2025. It covers applications filed with DNM (Dirección Nacional de Migraciones), permits granted, the gap between the two as a backlog proxy, and the throughput rate (grants divided by applications) as a measure of processing pressure. Before 2023, the system operated comfortably — throughput rates above 100% meant that more permits were issued each year than new applications arrived. Law 6984/22, which introduced mandatory temporary residence as a prerequisite for permanent residency for most applicants, structurally changed the pipeline starting in 2023: it pushed more filings into the temporary category, extended the overall timeline, and created a backlog that did not exist in the 2019-2022 period.
[...]
The per-nationality data, sourced from DNM grant records and published in the 2024 memory, shows that Brazil accounts for the largest single nationality share of grants (57.9% in 2024) — which is expected given the open border and established Brazilian community in the Alto Paraná region. But Germany (#3), Spain (#5), the Netherlands (#7), and the United States (#8) collectively represent a substantial non-Mercosur demand base that is growing. The investor pathway through SUACE (Law 5786/16) is a separate series: 269 applications in the first 11 months of 2024, versus 29,126 total applications for the full year — making investor residency less than 1% of total demand but strategically important for high-net-worth audiences.
[...]
None of this is static. DNM publishes new Memorias in the first quarter of each year, SUACE files are updated monthly, and the early signal from the January 2026 press release shows 2,817 applications in the first 20 days of the year — a 79% increase over the same 2025 period. The Momentum Index, which synthesizes these signals into a single score, rose from 59.5 in 2024 to 79.8 in 2025.

The dataset includes nationality-level counts for temporary applications, permanent applications, and total applications, along with a separate same-period table for temporary grants, permanent grants, and total grants.

        "description": "Sweden - Individual - Taxes on personal income 

 

 

 

# Sweden

 

 

## Employment income tax for residents

Employment income is taxed at the following rounded taxable income amounts (2026):

| Taxable income (SEK *) | National income tax (%) | Municipal income tax ** (%) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| From 0 to 643,000 | 0 | 32 |
| Over 643,000 | 20 | 32 |

* Swedish kronor

** This rate equals the average municipal tax rate.

## Employment income tax for non-residents

Non-residents working in Sweden for a Swedish employer or a foreign employer with a permanent establishment (PE) in Sweden are taxed a flat rate of 22,5% at source from 1 January 2026. From 1 January 2027 the taxation will be further reduced to 20%. The employee must apply for a SINK decision in order to benefit of the reduced rates. The same rate applies when a pension is paid by a Swedish source to a person not tax-resident in Sweden.

Non-residents working in Sweden for a non-Swedish employer without a PE in Sweden are tax liable in Sweden if the beneficiary of the employee’s work is an entity in Sweden and the work is performed under the management and control of the Swedish entity. The tax rate is 22,5%. An exception applies if the employees is working in Sweden for less than 15 days in a row and less than 45 days in total during a calendar year.

## Capital tax

Capital income is generally taxed at a flat rate of 30%. See Capital gains and investment income in the Income determination section for more information.

        "description": "Sweden taxes residents on worldwide income with municipal rates averaging about 32%, and a 20% state income tax applies on annual taxable income above SEK 625,800 for the 2025 income year. Two layers apply for residents. First, municipal income tax averages around 32% (varies by municipality). Second, the national state tax of 20% applies to the portion of taxable income above SEK 625,800 (2025). Sweden removed the higher 5% surtax years ago, which helps keep top marginal rates lower than a decade ago. With clear brackets and digital filing, planning salaries and bonuses is straightforward for employers and assignees.

## Is there a wealth or inheritance tax to worry about?

No wealth tax and no inheritance tax. However, property levies exist. For most private homes, the municipal property charge is 0.75% of the assessed value, capped at SEK 10,074 per building for the 2025 income year. Commercial property follows different rules.         "description": "Sweden operates under a dual-tier taxation model involving both national and municipal levies. Major taxes include income tax, corporate tax, value-added tax (VAT), social security contributions, property tax, environmental excise duties, and several targeted consumption taxes. Income tax remains the primary revenue source for municipalities. Domestic companies—defined as entities registered with the Swedish Companies Registration Office—are subject to national corporate taxation and payroll-related fees but are exempt from local income and net wealth taxes. Foreign-registered entities conducting business in Sweden face different obligations.
[...]
Personal income tax consists of municipal (local) and national components. Municipal rates vary by region, typically ranging from 29% to 35%, averaging around 32% nationwide. A progressive national surcharge applies to high earners. Total taxable income includes labor income—such as wages, self-employment, and farming profits—taxed progressively, and non-labor income—including dividends, interest, and capital gains—subject to a flat 30% rate. There is a distinction in treatment between Swedish residents and foreign workers, particularly regarding deductions and filing requirements.

[...]
#### Wealth and Property Taxes
[...]
Domestic companies are not liable for annual net wealth or property taxes. However, branches of foreign enterprises must pay property tax on owned or leased real estate used in business operations. Real estate owners—individuals or corporations—pay an annual property tax based on the assessed taxable value of the asset, ranging from 0.2% to 2.8%, now classified as a local tax collected by municipalities.",

Rate for:

# Net wealth tax and valuation discounts

Net wealth tax is a tax that must be paid to the municipality and the state. It’s assessed based on your net wealth. Net wealth is the value of your assets, such as real property, bank deposits, shares, and business capital after deductions have been made for valuation discounts and debt.

Valuation discounts reduce the taxable value of your wealth, and they are calculated automatically in the tax return.

The valuation discount only includes assets owned directly by the taxpayer.

Such asset may be housing and commercial property, shares, etc. and fixed assets.

Select year 2026 2025 2024 2023 2022 2021 2020 2019 2018 2017 2016 2015 2014 2013 2012 2011 2010 2009 2008 2007

| Tax class | Wealth | Rate |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tax class 0 and 1 | NOK 0 - 1,900,000 | 0.0 % |
| NOK 1,900,001 and above | 0.35 % |

The activation thresholds are for single taxpayers. For spouses, registered partners or spouse-equivalent cohabiting partners who are assessed jointly on their joint wealth, the activation thresholds are twice the amounts shown in the table.

        "description": "If you're a tax resident of Norway, you'll generally be liable for tax on all your income and wealth in Norway and abroad. In Norway, we pay tax on the wealth that we have as of 31 December in each calendar year. This wealth could for example consist of bank deposits, shares, cars and/or real property. You must pay wealth tax both to the municipality and to the State.
[...]
Wealth tax is calculated on the basis of your net wealth. Your net wealth is the wealth you have after deductible debt has been deducted.
[...]
The amount of wealth tax that you have to pay depends on the size of your wealth. See the tables and choose year.",

more targeted. It now plays a more important

        "url": "https://efremtid.no/en/rozliczenia-osobiste/formuesskatt/",
        "title": "Norwegian Wealth Tax (Formuesskatt) – what is it and who pays it? 2025 - Efremtid.no - official assistance in Norway",
        "description": "Wealth tax (Norwegian: formuesskatt) is a Norwegian tax levied on an individual's net wealth at the end of the tax year. It includes the value of assets owned minus debts - for example, funds in bank accounts (bankinnskudd), shares (aksjer), vehicles (e.g. a car) and real estate (fast eiendom). This tax is paid annually if the net wealth exceeds a set tax-free limit. Formuesskatt in Norway is levied at two levels: part of the tax goes to the municipality and part to the state budget.
[...]
), a so
[...]
formue is created – taxable assets.
[...]
att therefore applies to wealthier individuals
[...]
limit for a single person, see
[...]
). People with lower assets do not pay this tax at all, because their formae is below the exemption threshold.
[...]
## Tax-free amount and tax relief rates (2023–2025)
[...]
The Norwegian wealth tax has a tax-free amount (fribeløp, also known as grensebeløp or bunnfradrag) and progressive tax rates. Here are the thresholds and rates for 2023, 2024 and 2025:
[...]
- 2023: The tax-free amount is NOK 1 for a single person (for married couples filing jointly – a total of NOK 700). Net wealth up to this amount is tax-free. The excess above NOK 000 million is taxed at a rate of 3% (including 400% municipal tax and 000% state tax). Additionally, a higher state rate applies to the part of the wealth exceeding NOK 1,7 million, which increases the total tax to 1,0% on the excess above NOK 0,7 million (0,3% municipal + 20% state).
[...]
- 2024: The thresholds and rates remain unchanged compared to 2023. The tax-free amount remains NOK 1 (NOK 700 for couples). The tax rate for the excess above the threshold is 000% (3% municipality + 400% state), and for the part of the assets exceeding NOK 000 – 1,0% (0,7% + 0,3%).
[...]
- 2025: In 2025, the tax-free amount was increased to NOK 1 (for spouses, a total of NOK 760). The division of rates between the municipality and the state was also changed - the municipal tax was reduced to a maximum of 000%, while the state tax was increased to 3% on the excess of assets up to ~NOK 520 million and 000% for assets above ~NOK 0,525 million. However, the total rate remained at 0,475% of the value of assets above the tax-free amount (and 20,7% for the part exceeding the second threshold of ~NOK 0,575 million).
[...]
It is worth emphasizing that the above rates combine municipal and state tax. The municipal part rate was 0,7% for the years 2023-2024, and 2025% from 0,525. The state part rates in the years 2023-2024 were 0,3% for assets above the basic threshold and an additional 0,1 percentage points (i.e. 0,4% in total) for the part of assets above NOK 20 million. In 2025, the state rates increased to 0,475% and 0,575% (respectively for the range above the basic threshold and for assets > NOK 20,7 million) while the municipal rate was reduced.
[...]
Wealth tax will be calculated only on this excess. In 2025, a rate of 1,0% applies for wealth above the threshold (up to NOK 20 million). Mr. Jan will therefore pay an annual wealth tax of approx. 1 900 NOK (this is 1% of NOK 190). This amount is divided into municipal and state tax - 000% (NOK 0,525) will go to the municipal budget, and 997,5% (NOK 0,475) to the state budget. If Mr. Jan's assets did not exceed NOK 902,5 million, he would not pay formuesskatt at all (the tax-free amount protects people with smaller assets from this tax).",

        "url": "https://taxsummaries.pwc.com/norway/individual/taxes-on-personal-income",
        "title": "Norway - Individual - Taxes on personal income",
        "description": "To simplify reporting and compliance for temporary employment in Norway, a pay-as-you-earn (PAYE) system is introduced and in force from 1 January 2019. The PAYE scheme is applicable for limited tax liable non-resident workers who have a salary below bracket tax level 3, except offshore workers and seamen. For 2025, the annual salary threshold was 700,000 Norwegian kroner (NOK). It is agreed that the threshold will be NOK 725,050 in 2026. The rate is a flat 25%, including social security contributions.
[...]
The Norwegian income tax system for individuals is based on a dual tax base system: general income and personal income.
[...]
General income is taxed at a flat rate of 22%. The general income tax base comprises all categories of taxable income (i.e. income from employment, business, and capital). Tax allowances, expenses, and certain losses are deductible when computing general income. The taxes on general income are the county tax, the municipal tax (Norway is divided into 11 counties and subdivided into 356 municipalities), and the state tax.
[...]
## Bracket tax on personal income
[...]
Personal income between NOK 226,100 and NOK 318,299 is subject to a bracket tax of 1.7%.
[...]
For personal income between NOK 318,300 and NOK 725,049, the bracket tax rate is 4.0%.
[...]
For personal income between NOK 720,050 and NOK 980,099, the bracket tax rate is 13.7%.
[...]
For personal income between NOK 980,100 and NOK 1,467,199, the bracket tax rate is 16.8%.
[...]
For income exceeding NOK 1,467,200, the bracket tax rate is 17.8%.
[...]
The personal income tax (PIT) base comprises income mainly from employment, including benefits in kind, and pensions, as well as income from a self-proprietorship and certain remuneration from partnerships.",
        "position": 6
      },
      {
        "url": "https://lifeinnorway.net/income-tax",
        "title": "Income Tax in Norway 2025",
        "description": "In 2025, the standard deduction for employment income is set at 46% of the income, with an upper limit of NOK 92,000. For pension income, the standard deduction is 40%, with an upper limit of NOK 73,150.
[...]
## Income Tax Rates in Norway in 2025
[...]
Income tax in Norway is split into a base rate and a step tax, allowing for progressive taxation.
[...]
The**base rate**(*alminnelig inntekt*) of income tax in Norway is**22%**. Those who live inFinnmark or Nord-Tromswill pay a reduced rate of18.5%.
[...]
In addition, there is a so-called**step tax**(*trinnskatt*), sometimes calledbracket tax, which is a progressive tax applied at different income levels.
[...]
For the first**NOK 217,400**of your personal income, you will not pay any step tax. After this, the following rates apply:
[...]
* **1.7%**step tax on income between**NOK 217,401 and NOK 306,050**
* **4.0%**step tax on income between**NOK 306,051 and NOK 697,150**
* **13.7%**step tax on income between**NOK 697,151 and NOK 942,400**(*reduced for residents of Finnmark and Nord-Troms*)
* **16.7%**step tax on income between**NOK 942,40 and NOK 1,410,750**
* **17.7%**step tax on all income above**NOK 1,410,751**
[...]
These higher tax brackets have been adjusted in recent years, leading to increased taxation for high-income earners in Norway.
[...]
For 2025, the standard**national insurance contribution rate**is**7.9%**of personal income. If your income is below NOK 69,650, you do not pay any contributions. Therates for self-employed peopleare different.
[...]
If maths isn't your thing or you have unique individual circumstances, check out theincome tax calculatoroffered by the Norwegian Tax Administration. Simply fill in your estimated income, wealth and any relevant deductions. You'll then see an estimate of the tax you have to pay.
[...]
For example, a single person born in 1981 earning**NOK 700,000**would pay a total of**NOK 181,320**in tax deductions, which includes NOK 53,900 social security payment. The same person earning**NOK 1,200,000**would pay a total of**NOK 406,048**.
[...]
These examples do
[...]
take into account any personal circumstances such as property ownership,
[...]
and interest payments, personal wealth etc
[...]
So, on the NOK 700,000 salary, the snnual tax deduction is approximately 25.9% On the NOK 1,200,000 salary, it’s 33.8%.**But we’re not quite finished yet!**
[...]
There’s also a controversial**wealth tax**. This is well worth reading about if you are a high earner, with an annual income of at least NOK 1.2 million.",
        "position": 7
      },
      {
        "url": "https://www.sailglobal.com/tax-guides/no/norway-tax-guides-en",
        "title": "Norway Tax Guide 2025: Corporate & Personal Tax - SailGlobal",
        "description": "Taxes in Norway fall into two broad categories: direct and indirect. Key levies include corporate and personal income taxes, wealth and property taxes, social security contributions, value-added tax (VAT), excise duties, customs, and environmental taxes. Corporate income tax is assessed annually, with advance payments typically due on February 15 and April 15, based on the previous year’s taxable income. This system helps maintain steady revenue flow and supports fiscal planning.
[...]
Both domestic and foreign-owned businesses are subject to the same corporate income tax rate, which was reduced from 23% to 22% in 2019—a rate that remains unchanged through 2025. Partnerships and limited partnerships are not taxed at the entity level; instead, profits pass through to individual partners, who report them on their personal tax returns. A notable exception applies to oil and gas enterprises, which face an additional special petroleum tax. Since 2017, this surcharge has brought the total effective tax rate for petroleum activities to 78%, combining the standard corporate rate and the 54% special tax—among the highest in the world for resource extraction.
[...]
Personal income tax follows a progressive structure. As of 2025, the national base rate stands at 22%, though residents of Tromsø and Finnmark benefit from a reduced municipal rate of 18.5%. Additional brackets apply for higher earners, with surcharges based on income levels and net wealth, reflecting Norway’s commitment to equity and redistribution.
[...]
#### Inheritance and Gift Tax
[...]
In a move to support family-run businesses and intergenerational wealth transfer, Norway abolished inheritance and gift taxes in 2014. This reform has encouraged long-term business continuity and private investment, particularly among rural and SME owners. While no direct tax applies, large transfers may still be reviewed for potential capital gains implications if assets are later sold.
[...]
Norway also collects various niche taxes aimed at public health, environmental protection, and resource management. These include taxes on cosmetics, mineral water, pet food, and carbon emissions. Property owners face wealth taxation on real estate and unlisted shares exceeding certain thresholds—part of broader net wealth assessments conducted annually.",
        "position": 8
      },
      {
        "url": "https://lifeinnorway.net/wealth-tax",
        "title": "Wealth Tax in Norway (2025 Figures)",
        "description": "If you live in Norway, your global net worth is subject to wealth tax. But how does it work, and what exemptions apply? Here’s everything you need to know about Norway’s wealth tax in 2025.
[...]
However, unlike many other countries, Norway also taxes personal wealth. This means that in addition toincome tax, individuals with significant assets are required to pay tax on their net worth.
[...]
The wealth tax is particularly relevant for business owners and high-net-worth individuals who may have substantial assets but draw little or no salary, thereby reducing their income tax burden. Wealth tax ensures that even those who don’t rely on a traditional salary still contribute to society.
[...]
Crucially, Norway’s wealth tax applies to an individual’s global assets, regardless of where they are held.
[...]
Norway's wealth tax is easy to understand, although it does change from time to time. The following numbers apply to the 2025 tax year.
[...]
The total value of a person's worldwide**net wealth**above NOK 1.76 million (approx $170,000) is taxed at 1%. This threshold has been increased from NOK 1.7 million in 2023.
[...]
To be clear, that means that if someone owns net assets worth NOK 2 million, they will pay 1% tax on NOK 300,000, not the full NOK 2 million.
[...]
Of the wealth tax take, 0.475% goes to the state, with the remaining 0.525% going to the municipality in which the individual lives. This share has changed in recent years in favour of the state. In 2024, just 0.3% went to the state, with 0.7% going to the municipality.
[...]
When net wealth exceeds NOK 20.7 million, the marginal tax rate increases by 0.1%. Any additional revenue from this extra marginal rate goes to the state.
[...]
Net wealth is defined on the Norwegian tax return by the total value of assets minus total debt. So if you own a home worth NOK 5 million but are still paying off a mortgage, you won't be charged wealth tax on the full value of the house.
[...]
For wealth tax purposes, your primary dwelling is valued at 25% of its estimated market value. This means most homeowners don't  a lot of wealth tax simply through owning a home in which they live.
[...]
The thresholds given above are for single taxpayers. For spouses or registered partners who are assessed jointly for tax purposes, the thresholds for wealth tax are twice the amounts given above.
[...]
Norway, residents of Finnmark and the rural parts of Troms county receive an extra deduction on their personal tax returns. But those residents still have to pay wealth tax
[...]
**Taking on loans:**Borrowing against assets, such as property, can reduce taxable wealth since real estate is typically taxed at only 25-30% of its market value.
[...]
**Pension savings:**Contributions to an**Individual Pension Savings Account (IPS)**are exempt from wealth tax.
[...]
**Strategic investments:**Certain assets, such as primary homes (taxed at 25% of market value), holiday homes (30%), and mutual funds (75%), are taxed at lower rates. Artwork under NOK 1 million is not included in net wealth calculations.",

        "title": "Norway Tax Rates & System (2025) — TaxAtlas — TaxAtlas",
        "description": "Norway operates a dual income tax system that separates ordinary income (taxed at a flat 22%) from personal income subject to an additional progressive bracket tax (trinnskatt) reaching up to 17.6%. The combined top marginal rate on employment income is approximately 47.4%. As a major oil-producing nation, Norway funds its generous welfare state through the Government Pension Fund Global (the world's largest sovereign wealth fund) and maintains high social contributions and a 25% standard VAT.

In addition, earned income (employment and self-employment) is subject to a progressive bracket tax (trinnskatt) with rates ranging from 1.7% to 17.6% across five brackets. The bracket tax rates shown above are additive to the 22% base rate. The combined top marginal rate on employment income is approximately 47.4% (22% + 17.6% + 7.8% social security). A personal allowance (personfradrag) of NOK 88,250 reduces the ordinary income base.

        "title": "Czech Republic - Individual - Taxes on personal income",
        "description": "Czech Republic - Individual -
[...]
Czech tax residents are generally subject to Czech income tax on their worldwide income. Tax non-residents are generally taxed only on income considered Czech-source income.
[...]
## Personal income tax rates
[...]
The Czech Republic applies progressive taxation on income of tax residents as follows:
[...]
- Gross annual income up to CZK 1,762,812 (the bracket is calculated as 36x average monthly salary) is subject to a 15% rate.
- Gross annual income exceeding this threshold is subject to a rate of 23%.
[...]
The above rates apply to all types of income, except for income that has already been taxed by the final Czech withholding tax (WHT) at source (such as dividends or interest from bonds distributed by Czech companies) and except for certain types of foreign income included in the separate tax base (see below).
[...]
A special tax base with the rate of 15% applies to selected types of non-Czech investment income (e.g. dividends and interest from bonds from abroad).
[...]
Individuals can choose to include capital income from abroad in this separate tax base to which a flat 15% tax rate applies. However, tax allowances or tax-deductible items cannot be applied to reduce this tax base. The individual can thus decide whether to leave the capital income from abroad in the general tax base, which is subject to the progressive 15% and 23% rates but which can be reduced by tax allowances and deductible items, or to include this income in the separate tax base, which is subject to the flat 15% tax rate but cannot be reduced by tax allowances and deductible items.
[...]
There are no local taxes on income in the Czech Republic.",

        "title": "Czech Republic Personal Income Tax Rates (2025) — TaxAtlas — TaxAtlas",
        "description": "Czech Republic Personal Income Tax Rates (2025) — TaxAtlas — TaxAtlas
[...]
The Czech Republic applies a two-bracket income tax system: 15% on income up to 36 times the average salary (approximately CZK 1,582,812 per year in 2024) and 23% on income above this threshold. The higher rate was introduced in 2024, replacing a temporary solidarity surcharge. A basic taxpayer allowance of CZK 30,840 reduces the tax liability (equivalent to 15% of CZK 205,600). Additional credits exist for spouses, children, and disability.
[...]
| Income Range (CZK) | Tax Rate |
| --- | --- |
| Kč 0 – Kč 1.6M | 15% |
| Kč 1.6M+ | 23% |
[...]
An individual is a Czech tax resident if they have a permanent home in the Czech Republic or are present for at least 183 days in a calendar year. Residents are taxed on worldwide income. Non-residents are taxed only on Czech-source income.
[...]
The Czech Republic abolished the super-gross salary concept in 2021, which had effectively taxed employment income at a higher rate by adding employer social contributions to the tax base. Small business owners and freelancers (OSVČ) can opt for lump-sum expense deductions (30-80% of revenue depending on the type of activity) instead of tracking actual expenses. A lump-sum tax regime (paušální daň) is available for small entrepreneurs with annual income up to CZK 2 million.",

        "title": "Poland Tax Rates & System (2025) — TaxAtlas — TaxAtlas",
        "description": "Poland operates a two-bracket progressive income tax system with rates of 12% and 32%, alongside a popular 19% flat tax option for business income. The Polish Deal reforms of 2022 significantly restructured the tax landscape, increasing the tax-free allowance to PLN 30,000 and eliminating the ability to deduct health insurance contributions. Poland features a 19% corporate tax rate (9% for small businesses), a standard 23% VAT, and relatively high social security contributions.
[...]
Poland applies a progressive personal income tax (PIT) with two brackets: 12% on income between PLN 30,001 and PLN 120,000, and 32% on income above PLN 120,000. A tax-free allowance (kwota wolna od podatku) of PLN 30,000 per year applies, meaning no income tax is paid on the first PLN 30,000. The Polish Deal reforms (Polski Ład) implemented in 2022 raised the tax-free threshold from PLN 8,000 and lowered the first bracket from 17% to 12%. Entrepreneurs can opt for a 19% flat tax (podatek liniowy) on business income instead.
[...]
| Income Range | Tax Rate |
| --- | --- |
| zł0 – zł30K | 0% |
| zł30K – zł120K | 12% |
| zł120K+ | 32% |
[...]
Poland levies corporate income tax (CIT) at a standard rate of 19%. Small taxpayers (revenue up to EUR 2 million equivalent in PLN) and newly established companies benefit from a reduced rate of 9%. Poland has introduced an Estonian CIT option (estonski CIT or CIT-e) where tax is deferred until profits are distributed, with effective rates of approximately 20% for small companies and 25% for large companies upon distribution.
[...]
Capital gains from the sale of shares and financial instruments are taxed at a flat rate of 19% (podatek od zysków kapitałowych, often called 'podatek Belki'). This applies to listed and unlisted shares, bonds, derivatives, and investment fund units. Real estate gains are taxed at 19% if sold within 5 years of acquisition (calendar years), with exemptions for reinvestment in own housing purposes within 3 years.
[...]
Poland applies a standard VAT rate of 23%, with reduced rates of 8% and 5% for essential goods and services. The Polish VAT system follows EU directives. Poland has introduced mandatory split payment mechanisms for certain transactions and a national e-invoicing system (KSeF - Krajowy System e-Faktur) that will become mandatory. A VAT exemption applies to small businesses with annual turnover below PLN 200,000.

        "description": "Austria operates a progressive income tax system with rates ranging from 0% to 55%, featuring one of Europe's highest top marginal rates. The country levies a 23% corporate tax rate (reduced from 25% in 2024), a standard 20% VAT, and a 27.5% flat rate on capital income. Austria is known for its generous social benefits funded by substantial employer and employee social security contributions totaling approximately 40% of gross salary.

Austria applies a progressive income tax (Einkommensteuer) with seven brackets ranging from 0% to 55%. The top rate of 55% applies to income exceeding €1 million. Austria reformed its brackets in 2023-2024 to combat bracket creep caused by inflation, with thresholds adjusted accordingly. Employees benefit from various tax credits including the employee tax credit (Verkehrsabsetzbetrag) of €421 and the sole earner/single parent credit.
[...]

| €67K – €99K | 48% |
| €99K – €1.0M | 50% |
| €1.0M+ | 55% |
[...]
Austria levies corporate income tax (Körperschaftsteuer) at a flat rate of 23%, reduced from 25% effective 2024. A minimum corporate tax of €1,750 per year applies to GmbHs (limited liability companies) and €3,500 for AGs (joint stock companies). The minimum tax is creditable against future tax liabilities. Austria applies the participation exemption for dividends and capital gains from qualifying international subsidiaries (minimum 10% holding for at least one year).
[...]
Capital gains from financial investments (shares, bonds, derivatives) are subject to a flat withholding tax (Kapitalertragsteuer - KESt) of 27.5%. Real estate capital gains are also taxed at a flat 30% (Immobilienertragsteuer). The 27.5% rate replaced the previous system of taxing gains only on shares acquired after 2011. Austrian banks withhold KESt automatically on domestic securities transactions.

        "title": "Poland - Individual - Taxes on personal income (2026)",
        "description": "Polish tax residents pay PIT on their worldwide income. Non-residents are subject to Polish PIT on their Polish-sourced income only.
[...]
## Personal income tax rates
[...]
General PIT rules provide for the rates shown in the following table:
[...]
| Annual taxable income (PLN) | Tax rate |
| --- | --- |
| Over | Not over |
| 0 | 120,000 | 12% of the base less the amount decreasing tax (PLN 3,600)* |
| 120,000 | PLN 10,800 + 32% excess over PLN 120,000 |
[...]
* In the case of income up to PLN 120,000, the tax is 12% minus the amount decreasing tax, which is PLN 3,600 (i.e. 12% of PLN 30,000, which is the tax-free amount of income).
[...]
The tax-free amount is set at PLN 30,000. This means that taxpayers earning less than PLN 30,000 per year will be exempt from paying tax.
[...]
In the case of income exceeding PLN 120,000, the tax is PLN 10,800 + 32% of the excess over PLN 120,000. The decreasing tax amount is already included in the above sum of PLN 10,800.
[...]
### Taxation of income from business activity
[...]
Individuals running business activities (as sole traders or as partners in partnerships) can, instead of being subject to the tax scale, opt for a flat 19% income tax rate, lump-sum tax, or the so-called 'tax card'**, subject to certain conditions.
[...]
### Taxation of capital gains
[...]
Capital gains (including dividend and interest income) are taxed at a flat rate of 19%. The tax-free amount does not apply to this income.
[...]
As of 2023, the rental income is taxed only as a lump-sum tax on recorded revenues (8.5% rate for revenues below PLN 100,000 per year and 12.5% on the surplus over PLN 100,000). This means that the total rental income will be taxed without the right to deduct costs of earning income (e.g. maintenance costs, utilities).
[...]
Specified types of income, if gained by non-residents, are subject to special treatment. Namely, they are taxed at a flat rate of 20% calculated on revenue (cost deductions are not allowed) unless a double tax treaty (DTT) between Poland and the individual’s country of residence provides otherwise. These types of earnings include the following:
[...]
As of 1 January 2019, individuals who derive in a tax year income exceeding PLN 1 millionare required topay solidarity tax at the rate of 4% on the excess of this amount. An obligation of submitting a separate tax declaration by 30 April of the following tax year will also apply.",

        "url": "https://www.internations.org/paraguay-expats/spaniards",
        "title": "Spaniards in Paraguay - Find Jobs, Events & other Expats",
        "description": "# Looking for Spanish Expats in Paraguay?
[...]
## InterNations – Uniting Spaniards living in Paraguay
[...]
Would you like to get in touch with other Spaniards in Paraguay? Are you in need of help and advice from your Spanish compatriots? Or would you like to join other Spanish expats on an excursion to discover sights and landmarks – e.g. the Chaco in the west?
[...]
Whether you have settled as an expat in Paraguay or are still living in Spain and planning your upcoming foreign assignment, InterNations Paraguay offers everything you need: Established in 2007, our trusted community soon became the leading platform for international networking among expatriates and global minds around the world. Our network also counts a considerable number of Spanish expats in Paraguay, coming from all corners of Spain - from Barcelona, over Madrid to Sevilla.
[...]
## Find Tips and Advice on Paraguay from Spanish Expats
[...]
Of course, expat living in Paraguay is not just about shipping your belongings and filing in some bureaucratic papers. Regular socializing with fellow Spaniards is an essential part of expat life and helps getting accustomed to the Paraguayan way of life and mentality. InterNations helps you connect and meet up with other Spaniards in Paraguay, residing in Asunción, Ciudad del Este, Formosa and many other places throughout the country. Just take a look at our group of Spaniards in Asunción.
[...]
- Look up our top quality network to get to know open-minded expats from Spain in Paraguay.
- Obtain and share insider tips in our Paraguay Expat Forums – e.g. how to hire a babysitter fluent in your native tongue for your children or where to get paella in Ciudad del Este.
- Search the InterNations Places database for Paraguay to get information on institutions from your home country, including the Spanish embassy.
[...]
From the Chaco in the west to the Paraná region, we’ll help you connect with supportive and friendly Spanish expats in Paraguay.
[...]
## Join Our Expat Activities and Mingle with Fellow Spaniards in Paraguay
[...]
Moreover, please feel free to attend our regular expat events and activities in Paraguay. Our get-togethers are very diverse and range from a cozy gathering to enjoy the traditional Paraguayan sopa paraguaya, to big expat celebrations with hundreds of participants in Asunción’s best locations. You can also participate in one of our expat trips to go and see Encarnación and its Jesuit missions as well as other beautiful landmarks in Paraguay.
[...]
Our meet-ups and activities are a great opportunity to mingle with expats from Spain and various other countries in a casual setting. Interested? Just sign up and you’ll soon be receiving a cordial ¡Hola! from our Spaniards living in Paraguay!
[...]
Spanish Expats in Asunción
[...]
Living in Paraguay
[...]
## Communities in Paraguay
[...]
Spanish Expats in Asunción
[...]
Living in Paraguay",

        "url": "https://www.internations.org/paraguay-expats/spanish/es",
        "title": "Españoles en Paraguay - expatriados Paraguayos en Paraguay",
        "description": "¿Te gustaría contactar con otros españoles en el Paraguay? ¿Necesitas ayuda y consejos de compatriotas españoles? ¿O te gustaría unirte a otros expatriados en una excursión para descubrir lugares de interés y monumentos – como por ejemplo, el Gran Chaco en el oeste? Tanto si ya te has asentado como expatriado en el Paraguay o todavía estás viviendo en España y planeas tu próxima misión en el extranjero, InterNations Paraguay te ofrece todo lo que necesitas. Establecida en el 2007, nuestra comunidad pronto se convirtió en la plataforma líder en la creación de redes internacionales conectando a expatriados y gente de mentalidad global en todo el mundo. Nuestra red cuenta también con un número considerable de expatriados españoles en el Paraguay, procedentes de todos los rincones de España - desde Barcelona, Madrid hasta Sevilla.
[...]
Obviamente, embarcarse en la vida de expatriado en el Paraguay no solamente consiste en hacer maletas y encargarse de todo el papeleo. El intercambio regular con otros compatriotas españoles es también un aspecto importante en la experiencia del expatriado y puede ayudarte a que te familiarices con la cultura paraguaya. InterNations te ayuda a conocer e interactuar con otros españoles en el Paraguay, viviendo en Asunción, Formosa, Ciudad del Este y en muchos otros lugares del país. Sólo tienes que visitar nuestra red de españoles en Asunción. Por ejemplo, si de vez en cuando te apetece hablar en tu lengua materna, averiguar dónde encontrar comida española, o tener una charla sobre el último partido de tu equipo favorito, en la Comunidad paraguaya de InterNations podrás: - Navegar por nuestra red de alta calidad de expatriados para encontrar a gente que comparta tus intereses provenientes de España en el Paraguay. - Recibir y compartir consejos en nuestro Foro de Expatriados de Paraguay – p. ej. Cómo hallar a una niñera que hable tu lengua materna o donde encontrar comida típica de tu región en Asunción… - Explorar la base de datos de los Lugares de InterNations de el Paraguay para encontrar instituciones como la embajada española más cercana y muchos más. Desde la región de Paraná hasta el Gran Chaco en el oeste, te pondremos en contacto con amigables expatriados viviendo en el Paraguay. Por ejemplo, si de vez en cuando te apetece hablar en tu lengua materna, averiguar dónde encontrar comida española, o tener una charla sobre el último partido de tu equipo favorito, en la Comunidad paraguaya de InterNations podrás: - Navegar por nuestra red de alta calidad de expatriados para encontrar a gente que comparta tus intereses provenientes de España en el Paraguay. - Recibir y compartir consejos en nuestro Foro de Expatriados de Paraguay – p. ej. Cómo hallar a una niñera que hable tu lengua materna o donde encontrar comida típica de tu región en Asunción… - Explorar la base de datos de los Lugares de InterNations de el Paraguay para encontrar instituciones como la embajada española más cercana y muchos más. Desde la región de Paraná hasta el Gran Chaco en el oeste, te pondremos en contacto con amigables expatriados viviendo en el Paraguay.

      {
        "url": "https://residenciaparaguay.com/residencia-para-espanoles/",
        "title": "Residencia Paraguaya para españoles: Beneficios y requisitos",
        "description": "Aprovecha las ventajas si eres español de contar con una residencia temporal en Paraguay. ¡No pierdas más tiempo! Contáctanos y descubre cómo podemos ayudarte con tu residencia paraguaya.
[...]
Nosotros te brindamos asistencia para obtener tu Residencia Temporal en Paraguay, con el fin de que puedas alcanzar la calidad de vida que deseas al emigrar a este país. Nos encargamos de satisfacer todas tus necesidades y objetivos.
[...]
Ventajas de la
[...]
idencia temporal para españoles
[...]
* Para obtener la residencia permanente en Paraguay, es necesario primero solicitar la residencia temporal y luego esperar un período de 2 años para poder solicitar la residencia permanente. La residencia temporal te otorga la posibilidad de vivir legalmente en Paraguay desde el momento en que presentes tu solicitud, realizar actividades legales en el país y obtener la cédula de identidad paraguaya durante ese mismo período.
[...]
- Proceso fácil y suele completarse en 3 meses.
- Todos los países son elegibles para solicitar.
- Puedes optimizar tus ingresos al 0% de impuestos en determinados casos.
- Sólo debe realizar 2 viajes a Paraguay.
- Las tarifas gubernamentales son relativamente bajas.
- Tiene el derecho de vivir y trabajar de forma indefinida en Paraguay.
- Se puede viajar sin visa dentro del Mercosur.
- Para mantener la residencia, es necesario no estar ausente del país por más de 3 años consecutivos, sin justificación.
- El costo de vida es muy barato.
- El idioma principal es el español.
[...]
* Para solicitar la residencia temporal en Paraguay, solo es necesario que nos proporciones los siguientes documentos indicados a continuación. Nosotros nos encargaremos de todo el trámite administrativo y organizaremos las citas necesarias. Solo tendrás que presentarte, firmar los documentos ¡y listo!
[...]

{"path": "/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/european_source_markets_research.md", "content": "# European Customer Source Markets for Paraguay Relocation — Research Report
## OUTSIDE Netherlands/Belgium

### Official DNM Data (Direccion Nacional de Migraciones) — 2025 Full Year
- Total applications: 47,687 (+63% YoY)
- Total granted: 40,600
- Brazil: 23,526 (58%)
- Argentina: 4,366
- **Germany: 1,652 (#3 globally)**
- **Spain: 1,023 (#5 globally)**
- Netherlands: 772
- USA: 736
- Russia: 509
- **France: 492**
- Venezuela: 847
- UK, Italy, Switzerland, Sweden, Poland, Czechia, Ireland: aggregated under "Other" in DNM year-end, but 2024 data shows: Italy (168 apps, 130 grants), UK (106 apps, 77 grants), Switzerland (87 apps, 63 grants), Poland (122 apps, 106 grants), Czechia (91 apps, 55 grants), Sweden (29 apps, 24 grants), Austria (79 apps, 78 grants)

---

## 1. GERMANY — PRIORITY #1

### Residency Applications
- 1,652 residencies granted in 2025 (3rd place globally, 1st in non-Latin America)
- 2024: 1,010 applications, 915 grants (91% approval rate)
- Growth: +63% YoY from 2024 to 2025
- Market share: ~4% of total applications (huge for a non-neighboring country)

### Tax Pressure Driving Exodus
- Income tax: Progressive 14-45%, plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge = top marginal ~47.5%
- No federal wealth tax, but some states have property taxes
- Inheritance tax: 7-50% depending on relationship, with only 20,000 EUR allowance for non-family
- Capital gains: 25% flat + surcharge (~26.375%)
- Social contributions: ~40% combined employer/employee
- Germany is the most tax-burdened EU economy for high earners

### Existing German-Language Competitors (WEAK competition for Nexa)
- **paraguayservice.de** — full-service German-language firm
- **sorgenfreiparaguay.com** — German couple who moved in 2018, comprehensive services, ~2,100 EUR
- **paraguayinfo.de** — German-language information portal
- **destination-paraguay.com/de/** — also French site, has German page
- **paraguay-penner.de** — "over 3,000 clients" in 35 years, Dominant but old-school
- **goodbyematrix.com** — German team, focuses on digital nomads, since 2017
- **goparaguay.de** — German-speaking Paraguayans
- **auswandernachparaguay.de** — 35+ years experience
- Assessment: MANY small German firms exist, but none dominate. Nexa already speaks German (DE is one of the 4 languages). Strong opportunity if Nexa positions against fragmented market.

### Addressable Market
- 1,652 annual applicants, growing 63% YoY
- Germany has ~2,000,000 millionaires (4th globally)
- ~1.5M Germans express desire to emigrate (official study)
- Estimated TAM: 5,000-8,000 German-speakers considering Paraguay annually
- Price range in market: 1,500-2,500 EUR

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Telegram "Auswandern nach Paraguay" | 2,938 members, very active |
| Telegram @paraguaypioniere | 2,740 subscribers |
| Skool "Auswandern nach Paraguay" | 198 members, weekly meetups |
| Facebook "Expats in Paraguay" | Largest English/FB group |
| Paraguay Forum (paraguay-forum.com) | German-language forum |
| YouTube "Paraguay Pioniere" | Active channel |
| auswandererforum.de | German emigration forum |
| YouTube "GoodbyeMatrix" | Targeting PT/digital nomads |
| Instagram/FB ads targeting "Auswandern" interests | Direct response |
| Key SEO terms: "Auswandern nach Paraguay", "Paraguay Aufenthaltsgenehmigung", "Paraguay Steuern" | High search volume |

---

## 2. UNITED KINGDOM — PRIORITY #2 (URGENT WINDOW)

### Residency Applications
- 106 applications in 2024, 77 grants
- Estimated 2025: ~150-200 applications (non-dom abolition effect kicking in)
- Small base but HIGH GROWTH trajectory predicted

### Tax Pressure — Non-Dom Abolition (Effective April 6, 2025)
- Income tax: 20/40/45% (basic/higher/additional)
- Inheritance tax: 40% on estates above 325,000 GBP (frozen until 2030)
- **NON-DOM ABOLITION**: Remittance basis abolished. From 2025, worldwide income taxed on arising basis. IHT now residence-based (10/20 year test). This is the biggest driver.
- 16,500 millionaires expected to leave UK in 2025 (Henley & Partners — highest globally)
- Capital gains: 10-24% (increased in 2025 Budget)
- UK has NO wealth tax — but the non-dom change affects ~14,800 individuals

### Existing English-Language Competitors (MANY)
- **paraguaysovereign.com** — Comprehensive English site, very professional
- **expatsettle.com** — UK-specific guides
- **destination-paraguay.com** — English + French + German
- **theparaguayresidency.com** — Global English service
- **citizenx.com** — US-focused but English
- **paraguaypathways.com** — English/Portuguese/Italian
- Assessment: VERY COMPETITIVE in English. Nexa already serves English speakers. Differentiation needed for UK-specific messaging.

### Addressable Market
- ~200,000 UK expats in tax-havens already; thousands more planning exit
- 16,500 millionaires leaving UK in 2025 (Henley & Partners)
- UK population of ~68M, 3M+ non-doms and high-net-worth affected
- Estimated TAM: 500-1,000 UK applicants to Paraguay within 3 years

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Facebook "Expats in Paraguay" | Largest FB group |
| Facebook "British Expats in South America" | ~5,000 members |
| Expat.com Paraguay forum | Active UK-specific threads |
| InterNations Brits in Paraguay | Community in Asunción |
| UK financial press (FT, ThisIsMoney, Telegraph) | Non-dom abolition coverage |
| Reddit r/expats, r/iwantout, r/digitalnomad | Regular Paraguay discussions |
| Henley & Partners reports | Wealth migration data |
| YouTube — Nomad Capitalist, IMI Advisor | Influencers in space |
| LinkedIn — targeting UK finance professionals | HNWI audience |
| Expat forums in Spain/Portugal (people comparing options) | Indirect channel |

---

## 3. FRANCE — PRIORITY #3

### Residency Applications
- 492 granted in 2025 (10th place, up from 185 grants in 2024)
- 2024: 259 applications, 185 grants
- Growth: +166% YoY (huge surge)
- Underestimated market — 472 grants places France at #10 globally

### Tax Pressure
- Income tax: Progressive 0-45% + social charges 17.2% = effective up to ~62%
- Wealth tax (IFI): Real estate assets >1.3M EUR, progressive 0.5-1.5% on real estate only
- Inheritance tax: 5-60% depending on relationship (children get 100K EUR allowance, then steep)
- Flat tax (PFU): 30% on investment income
- New 20% minimum tax for high earners (2025)
- France ranks among highest tax burdens in Europe

### Existing French-Language Competitors
- **destinationparaguay.fr** — French site, same firm as German/English
- **resident-paraguay.fr** — "1st French-language service", YouTube presence (Sebastien & Matt)
- **hastamerica.com** — French agency, Alain (owner)
- **easyparaguay.com/fr/** — French platform, $1,300 total
- **expatfacil.com** — French services
- **paraguay-resident.com.py** — French-speaking team, 2,500 EUR
- **connexionparaguay.com** — French full-service
- Assessment: SEVERAL French competitors but none dominant. Market growing fast.

### Addressable Market
- 492 annual applicants, growing 166% YoY
- France has 2.8M millionaires
- ~50,000 French people emigrate annually (highest in EU for certain categories)
- French-speaking global population (including Belgium/Switzerland/Canada) ~300M
- Estimated TAM: 1,000-1,500 French applicants to Paraguay annually

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Expat.com forum Paraguay (French) | Very active, "DEMANDES DE RESIDENCE AU PARAGUAY EN 2025" thread |
| YouTube "Resident Paraguay" | Sebastien & Matt, strong French following |
| Facebook "Expats in Paraguay" (French subset) | Major intersection |
| InterNations French in Paraguay | Active community |
| Facebook "Españoles Residentes en Paraguay" | Spanish/French cross |
| LinkedIn professional networks | French entrepreneurs |
| Expat blogs (ParaguayExpert, Hello Paraguay) | French-owned |
| Connexion France news | French expat media |

---

## 4. ITALY — HIGH POTENTIAL

### Residency Applications
- 2024: 168 applications, 130 grants
- Estimated 2025: ~250-300 applications
- Major historical Italian community in Paraguay (~30% of population has Italian ancestry)
- Bilateral dual citizenship treaty in place since 2019
- 150th anniversary of diplomatic relations celebrated in 2025

### Tax Pressure
- Income tax: 23/35/43% progressive (3 brackets), plus regional (1.23-3.33%) and municipal (0-0.8%) = effective up to ~47%
- Wealth tax: IVIE 0.76% on foreign real estate, IVAFE 0.2-0.4% on foreign financial assets
- Inheritance tax: Surprisingly low — 4% for spouse/children (1M EUR exempt), 6% siblings (100K exempt), 8% others
- Capital gains: 26% flat
- Lump-sum regime: 100K EUR/year for new residents (very attractive for HNWIs)
- **Key driver**: High tax evasion pressure, 43% top rate kicks in at just 50K EUR

### Existing Italian-Language Competitors
- **residenzainparaguay.com** — Italian site, "Studio Panama Italia", $2,500
- **reframed.it** — Italian relocation firm, ISO certified
- **legalmigrationparaguay.com/it/** — Italian legal services
- **ciaoparaguay.com** — Italian, full-service
- **livinginparaguay.com/it/** — Italian team, multiple visits
- **vivereparaguay.com** — Italian, comprehensive
- **paraguaypathways.com** — Offers Italian-language support
- Assessment: FEWER competitors than French market, moderate competition.

### Addressable Market
- ~250-300 annual applicants, with strong cultural ties
- 2.5M Paraguayans of Italian descent
- Italy has 1.3M millionaires
- 43% tax rate at just ~54K USD income drives emigration
- Estimated TAM: 500-1,000 Italian applicants to Paraguay within 3 years

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Facebook groups — "Españoles Residentes en Paraguay" | Cross-community |
| YouTube Italian expat channels | Growing, but few heavyweights |
| Italian financial blogs (REFramed, etc.) | Targeting fiscal emigrants |
| InterNations Italians in Paraguay | Community |
| Expat.com Italian section | Active |
| LinkedIn targeting Italian entrepreneurs | Specific outreach |
| Italian diaspora associations in Paraguay | Gruppo Noi Siamo (50K members) |
| Italian fiscal emigration forums | "Trasferirsi all'estero" communities |

---

## 5. SPAIN — HIGH VOLUME, LOW VALUE PER CLIENT

### Residency Applications
- 1,023 granted in 2025 (5th place globally)
- 2024: 463 applications, 391 grants
- Consistent growth, but many are retirees with modest means

### Tax Pressure
- Income tax: Progressive 19-47% (higher in some regions like Catalonia up to 54%)
- Wealth tax: 0.2-3.5% on net wealth over 700K EUR (700K exemption + 300K main home)
- Solidarity tax: Additional 1.7-3.5% on wealth >3M EUR (made permanent in 2025)
- Inheritance tax: 7.65-34% at national level, but Madrid/Andalusia have 99-100% exemptions
- Beckham Law: 24% flat for new residents (first 600K EUR) — attractive but temporary

### Existing Spanish-Language Competitors (HIGH competition)
- **residenciaparaguay.com** — Spanish-focused
- **guiaparaguayresidencia.info** — Spanish DIY guide
- **vivirparaguay.com** — Spanish community site
- **residenciafiscalenparaguay.com** — Spanish fiscal emigration
- Assessment: VERY COMPETITIVE in Spanish. Spanish firms are abundant and cheap. Low-margin market.

### Addressable Market
- 1,023 annual applicants, but lower average client value
- Spain-Paraguay double taxation treaty effective 2026 (first with OECD country!)
- Large Paraguayan community in Spain (~83K registered)
- Estimated TAM: High volume, low price sensitivity

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Facebook "Españoles Residentes en Paraguay" | Active community |
| InterNations Spaniards in Paraguay | Active |
| VivirParaguay.com | Spanish community |
| Expat.com Spanish forum | Active |
| YouTube — Spanish expat channels | Growing |
| Spanish fiscal emigration forums | "Cambiar residencia fiscal" |

---

## 6. SWITZERLAND — HIGH NET WORTH, MULTILINGUAL

### Residency Applications
- 2024: 87 applications, 63 grants
- Estimated 2025: ~120-150 applications
- Small volume but EXTREMELY HIGH NET WORTH per client

### Tax Pressure
- Income tax: Federal max 11.5% + cantonal (varies 12-36%) = effective up to ~36-45% depending on canton
- Wealth tax: 0.1-1% annually on net assets (cantonal) — rare in Europe, hurts HNWIs
- Inheritance tax: Varies by canton. Most exempt direct descendants. Unrelated heirs up to 50%.
- Capital gains on private assets: 0% (major advantage!)
- Key driver: Wealth tax still exists in Switzerland, pushing HNWIs to reconsider

### Existing Competitors (German/French/Italian overlap)
- German and French firms already serve Swiss clients
- **destination-paraguay.com** covers FR/DE/EN
- No Swiss-specific Paraguay relocation firms identified
- Assessment: UNDERSERVED for Swiss-specific messaging. Nexa already has DE, which covers Swiss-German, plus FR for Romandie.

### Addressable Market
- Small volume but very high value per client
- 15.7% of Swiss adults are millionaires (highest globally)
- Estimated TAM: 200-300 Swiss applicants to Paraguay annually at current trajectory

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| InterNations Swiss in Paraguay | Community |
| Swiss expat forums | EDAB admin |
| Sociedad Suiza Paraguaya | 250+ members, 140+ year history |
| German-language channels (overlap with DE) | DE content serves Swiss |
| LinkedIn — Swiss finance/wealth management professionals | HNWI targeting |

## 7. NORDIC COUNTRIES (SWEDEN, NORWAY, DENMARK, FINLAND) — UNDERSERVED NICHE

### Residency Applications
- Sweden 2024: 29 applications, 24 grants
- Norway: Not in top 20 (likely <20/year)
- Denmark: Estimated <20/year
- Finland: Not listed
- **Small volumes today but HIGH POTENTIAL due to extreme tax pressure**

### Tax Pressure (EXTREME)
| Country | Top Income Tax | Wealth Tax | Inheritance Tax | Notes |
|---------|---------------|------------|-----------------|-------|
| Sweden | ~52% (32% municipal + 20% state) | None (abolished 2007) | None (abolished 2005) | Capital gains 30% flat |
| Norway | ~47.4% (22% base + bracket up to 17.6% + 7.7% social) | **1.0% on net wealth >1.9M NOK** | None (abolished 2014) | Exit tax on shares |
| Denmark | ~56% (top in OECD) | None | None | High consumption taxes |
| Finland | ~51.4% (progressive) | None | None | |

- Norway's wealth tax is the PRIMARY driver: 1% on global net worth above ~$165K USD
- Sweden abolished wealth/inheritance taxes but income tax up to 52%
- Paraguay's 0% foreign income + 0% wealth tax = massive savings for Nordics

### Existing Competitors (ALMOST NONE in Nordic languages)
- **paraguaytax.com** has "Paraguay Tax Residency for Scandinavians" page
- **expatsettle.com** has Sweden/Denmark-specific guides
- **NO dedicated Swedish/Norwegian/Danish/Finnish-language firms** identified
- Assessment: EXTREMELY UNDERSERVED. No competitor serves these languages. Massive opportunity.

### Addressable Market
- Very small today but growing exponentially
- Norwegian millionaire exodus accelerating (wealth tax main driver)
- Most Nordics speak excellent English — English content works, but native-language would differentiate
- Estimated TAM: 100-300 Nordic applicants to Paraguay within 3 years

### Best Channels
| Channel | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Facebook "Scandinavians in South America" | Cross-Nordic group |
| InterNations Swedish/Norwegian/Danish in Paraguay | Small communities |
| Skatteetaten (Norway) / Skatteverket (Sweden) emigration info | Trigger for search |
| LinkedIn — Norwegian business owners (wealth tax exodus) | High-intent audience |
| Norwegian financial media — wealth tax debate | News coverage drives interest |
| YouTube expat channels in Swedish/Norwegian | Few exist — opportunity! |

---

## 8. EASTERN EUROPE (POLAND, CZECHIA, AUSTRIA) — GROWING

### Residency Applications (2024 data)
- Poland: 122 applications, 106 grants
- Czechia: 91 applications, 55 grants
- Austria: 79 applications, 78 grants
- Slovakia: 87 applications, 65 grants

| Czechia | 15-23% | None | Very low (exempt spouse/children) |
| Austria | 0-55% (1M+ EUR bracket) | None | 0-15% (low) |
| Slovakia | 19-25% | None | Low |

- Austria has 55% top rate on income >1M EUR (one of highest in Europe)
- Poland/Czechia have lower tax rates but growing political unrest is driving emigration

### Existing Competitors
- **paraguayjednoduse.cz** — Czech-language service, "trusted by 110+ clients"

- **getresidencyparaguay.com** — Has Czech-specific guide
- Assessment: Czechia has local competition (paraguayjednoduse.cz is strong). Poland has some. Austria has none.

### Best Channels

## STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATIONS FOR NEXA PARAGUAY

### Tier 1 (Immediate Opportunity)
1. **GERMANY** — Nexa already has DE language. Market is 1,652 applicants/year, growing 63%. Many small competitors but none dominant. Invest in German SEO/content. Target "Auswandern nach Paraguay" keywords. Unique selling point: Already serving Dutch/Belgian clients successfully.

2. **UK** — Non-dom abolition is an URGENT window. 16,500 millionaires leaving UK in 2025. Target UK financial advisors and expat forums. Content focus: "Non-dom abolition: Why Paraguay is the answer."

3. **FRANCE** — 492 applicants, growing 166% YoY. Several French competitors but market growing fast. Nexa doesn't currently serve France but should add FR language.

### Tier 2 (High Value, Lower Volume)
4. **SWITZERLAND** — Low volume but very high value. Wealth tax is unique pain point. DE content already covers Swiss-German.

5. **ITALY** — Strong cultural ties, growing market. Fewer competitors than French market.

6. **NORDIC COUNTRIES** — Almost zero competition. Wealth tax in Norway is a massive driver. English content works but native-language would dominate.

### Tier 3 (Volume Market)
7. **SPAIN** — High volume (1,023) but low margin. Competitors are cheap and numerous.

8. **EASTERN EUROPE** — Niche. Czechia has local competition. Austria is untapped.

### Competitive Advantage for Nexa
- Already has EN, NL, DE, ES (covers 4 of the 8 priority markets)
- Adding FR would unlock France
- Adding IT would unlock Italy
- For Nordics/Eastern Europe: English content currently sufficient but native-language would be differentiator
- Unique value: Combination of European understanding + Paraguay local presence
- Key differentiator: "Serving Europeans in their own language" vs generic English services

Searched DNM official migration data for 2025 figures, tax rates from PwC/TaxAtlas/OECD sources, competitor analysis in 6 languages (DE, FR, IT, ES, PT, EN), and identified specific community channels (Facebook groups, Telegram channels, YouTube channels, forums) for each country.

### Key Findings

**DNM 2025 Official Data (confirmed from migraciones.gov.py):**

- Spain #5: 1,023 granted
- France #10: 492 granted

**Top 3 Priority Markets for Nexa:**

2. **UK** (est. 150-200/yr) — URGENT WINDOW from non-dom abolition (April 2025). 16,500 millionaires leaving UK (Henley & Partners). English content is competitive but UK-specific targeting (non-dom pain points) is underserved. UK inheritance tax is 40%, non-dom regime gone.

3. **FRANCE** (492/yr, +166% YoY growth) — Several French competitors exist but market growing fastest. Nexa would need to add FR language. France has 45% top tax + 17.2% social charges + IFI wealth tax on real estate.

**Underserved Niches (Big Opportunity):**
- **Nordic countries** — Almost zero competition. Norway's 1% wealth tax is driving exodus. No Swedish/Norwegian/Danish-language firms exist.
- **Switzerland** — High net worth per client. Wealth tax (0.1-1%) unique to Switzerland is pain point. DE content already covers Swiss-German.
- **Italy** — Strong historical ties (30% of Paraguayans have Italian ancestry). Low inheritance tax (4%) isn't the driver — it's the 43% income tax kicking in at just ~54K USD.

**Competitor Landscape:**
- English market: VERY competitive (paraguaysovereign.com, expatsettle.com, etc.)
- German market: Fragmented, ~8-10 small firms, none dominant
- French market: Moderate competition (~7 firms), market growing fastest
- Italian market: Moderate (~6 firms), fewer than French
- Spanish market: Very competitive, low-margin
- Nordic/Eastern European: Almost zero native-language competition
